Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 30 Content: The Jai Hind Business Conclave 2023-24, hosted by Jai Hind College’s BBA department in partnership with TCS, brought together budding business leaders, industry experts, and students from across the country in a grand two-day event on December 15 and 16, 2023. Held at the college campus in Mumbai, the Conclave aimed to bridge the gap between education and the corporate world, providing students with immersive, real-world experiences. This year’s Conclave was a resounding success, with over 3000 attendees engaging in dynamic events, speaker sessions, and workshops. Sponsors and Media Partners Aequitas Investment served as the Title Sponsor, alongside co- sponsors i.e. Mutual Fund partner SBI Mutual Fund, Study Abroad Partner Riya Study Abroad, Gifting Partner Plum Body Lovin, Dessert Partner Belgian Waffle, Broadcast Partner Evepaper, Beverage Partner Coffee by Di Bella, Snacking Partner Shriram Papad, and Beauty Partner Lakme Academy. These brands added vibrancy to the event, with each contributing unique touchpoints, samples, and interactive experiences. Media partners, including Sachi Shiksha, Nava Bharat, and Sach Kahoon, extended the Conclave’s reach, capturing key moments and sharing highlights with a broader audience. Additionally, JBC was featured in online editions of newspapers like the Business Standard, The Print, The Hindustan Express, Mid-Day, Daily Hunt and ANI. Engaging Management Events The Conclave’s first day was a thrilling showcase of competitive spirit and innovative thinking, with participants taking part in five signature management events designed to challenge their business acumen, strategic thinking, and problem-solving skills. 1. Surviving on 0 Rupees 2.0 A continuation of last year’s flagship event, this game gave each team Rs. 50 as starting capital, which they were required to grow through buying, selling, and creative business transactions. The team with the highest earnings was crowned the winner. 2. Corporate Crucible A multi-round, elimination-based competition, Corporate Crucible required participants to prove their resilience, critical thinking, and agility through a series of intellectually demanding tasks. 30

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 31 Content: 3. BizTown Bonanza BizTown Bonanza was a life-sized game that combined luck with strategic decision-making, challenging players to take calculated risks, and leverage their resources to come out on top. 4. To Be Blunt This diplomatic debate event, structured in an MUN format, required teams to argue for or against a designated topic on behalf of their assigned country. 5. Risk Hai Toh Ishq Hai A high-stakes quiz game that required participants to weigh risk against reward, this event allowed teams to select question categories based on difficulty levels and corresponding monetary values. Insightful Speaker Sessions and Workshops The second day of the Conclave featured a series of enriching sessions with industry leaders, who shared insights into various facets of business and career growth. These sessions provided attendees with invaluable guidance on how to navigate the professional world, adapt to evolving industry demands, and balance innovation with tradition. 1. Arjun Vaidya – Founder of Dr. Vaidya and V3 Ventures, Mr. Vaidya discussed how to balance tradition with innovation in family businesses, sharing the journey of taking a heritage brand to a new- age marketplace. With his vast social media presence and experience, he managed to keep the students engaged throughout. 2.Anahita Kumar – As Legal Director at Kellogg, South Asia, Mrs. Kumar explored the complexities of navigating legal challenges in the food processing industry and provided a novel and intriguing point of view. 3. Manish Mehta – Joint President and National Head of Sales at Kotak Mahindra Asset Management Co. Ltd., Mr. Mehta demystified mutual funds, breaking down the fundamentals of investment for aspiring finance professionals and sharing his learnings from his long tenure working at one of the leading financial institutions in India. 4. Srinivas Jain – Executive Director of SBI Mutual Funds, Mr. Jain led a session on optimizing capital, addressing both the theoretical and practical aspects of fund management, risk assessment, and portfolio balancing. A clear insight from an industry leader, that truly inspired all the students,and especially the finance enthusiasts, in the audience. 31

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 32 Content: T H E F A M I L Y M A N A G E D B U S I N E S S H U B The day also featured an engaging workshop by Harsh and Hetvi Kamdar, founders of Trading Secrets, who provided an introduction to stock markets and technical analysis. This hands-on session enabled participants to better understand the dynamics of trading and investment strategies, equipping them with tools to navigate the stock market. JBC 2023-24 was a memorable celebration of learning, innovation, and collaboration, embodying the college’s commitment to nurturing future business leaders. With a diverse lineup of events and insightful sessions, the Conclave provided students with practical tools, knowledge, and industry connections to thrive in their future careers. As Jai Hind College looks forward to future editions, the Conclave remains a shining example of its dedication to fostering holistic education, preparing students to excel in the ever-evolving corporate world. THE FAMILY MANAGED BUSINESS HUB Faculty Advisor and Chairperson: Dr Rakhi Sharma Student Secretary: Aasiya Gheewala The Family Managed Business Hub at Jai Hind College serves as a dynamic platform aimed at nurturing and empowering aspiring entrepreneurs within family-run enterprises. With a vision to become a leading center for fostering entrepreneurial excellence, the Hub aspires to equip students with innovative strategies, strong leadership qualities, and sustainable business practices. 32

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 33 Content: Recognizing the unique challenges and opportunities inherent in such businesses, the hub provides tailored support, mentorship, and resources to equip students and alumni with the skills and knowledge necessary to succeed in the competitive business landscape. Its establishment underscores the College's commitment to fostering innovation and sustainability in family-owned enterprises, addressing a critical need in today's evolving economic landscape. On February 24, 2024, Jai Hind College (Autonomous) celebrated a momentous occasion in its rich history as it marked its 75th Anniversary with the inauguration of the Family Managed Business Hub, a groundbreaking initiative by the Bachelors of Management Studies (BMS) and Business Administration (BBA). This event not only underscored the college's commitment to fostering entrepreneurship but also heralded a new era of innovation and collaboration. The launch event was graced by an illustrious gathering of students, esteemed alumni, and industry leaders, all gathered to commemorate this significant milestone. Among the distinguished attendees was Mrs. Pooja Choudary, the esteemed owner and founder of Infinity Cars Pvt. Ltd. Mrs. Choudary's presence added an aura of prestige as she showcased the BMW 6GT and shared her inspiring entrepreneurial journey, emphasizing the importance of seizing opportunities and inspiring students to pursue their dreams relentlessly. Also present was Mr. Sam Samaya, the visionary owner and founder of the USA skincare brand Vitamins Sea & Beauty, who traveled from Washington DC to share insights into his remarkable business journey. Mr. Samaya's discourse illuminated the challenges of international relocation and underscored the indispensable role of teamwork in achieving remarkable success, leaving attendees captivated and inspired. The Networking Meet, a central highlight of the event, commenced with stirring addresses from esteemed figures including Dr. Vijay Dabholkar, the Principal of Jai Hind College, Dr. Rakhi Sharma, Head of the Management Department, and Aasiyah Gheewala, Student Secretary, alongside esteemed Chief Guests. Following these insightful speeches, attendees engaged in networking over high tea, facilitating the exchange of ideas, experiences, and knowledge among current and future family business owners. 33

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 34 Content: This unique gathering provided a fertile ground for participants to share their expertise and glean insights from one another, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation within the family business community. Additionally, attendees had the opportunity to reminisce and cherish fond memories through engaging activities such as a write-up banner and a photo wall, adding a nostalgic touch to the occasion. Behind the scenes, the Managing team, under the guidance of Academic head Dr. Rakhi Sharma, consists of Student Secretary Aasiyah Gheewala and Student Joint Secretaries. Additionally, the founding team, comprising Usman Gheewala, Hormuz Hansotia, Yogansh Banthia, Chinkal Tulsiani, Trisha Kanuga, Ishika Somani, and Adit Patel, played a pivotal role in bringing the Family Managed Business Hub to fruition. Their unwavering dedication and visionary leadership have laid a robust foundation for future generations of entrepreneurs to thrive and succeed, epitomizing the spirit of innovation and excellence that defines Jai Hind College. Furthermore, the event garnered widespread media coverage, with prominent publications such as Mumbai Mirror featuring the celebration. A press release about the event was disseminated across over 196 sites, including notable platforms such as Times of India and Google News, amplifying its reach and impact on a global scale. As the curtains closed on this historic event, the Jai Hind College community stood united in its commitment to nurturing the entrepreneurial spirit and driving positive change in the world of business. The launch of the Family Managed Business Hub signifies not just a milestone, but a promise of boundless opportunities and transformative growth in the years to come. 34

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 35 Content: NOTEWORTHY EVENTS CONCOURSE 2023 Faculty Advisor: Prof. Yasmin Singaporewala Student Members: Jaithra Sood , Karan Bathija, Vanshika Makhijani , Hiya Meghani, Tamanna Lillani , Pritika Mulchandani ConCourse, the prestigious speakers' committee of Jai Hind College, affiliated with the BAF, BBI and BFM Departments, is dedicated to bridging the gap between academia and the professional world. Our mission is to provide students with unparalleled exposure to experts across various industries, fostering a holistic approach to education that encourages both personal and intellectual growth. Over the years, ConCourse has become an integral part of the college experience, serving as a beacon of knowledge, insight and opportunity for students seeking to broaden their horizons. ConCourse consistently serves as a vibrant platform where distinguished professionals and luminaries from a wide range of industries converge. The rationale behind inviting such a diverse array of speakers is to ensure that students are exposed to multiple perspectives, enriching their understanding of various career paths and industries. These sessions are meticulously curated to cater to the intellectual curiosity of every student, offering them not just information but an immersive experience that delves into the intricacies of different professions. Our committee's emphasis on leveraging an extensive network of industry experts is rooted in the belief that real-world exposure is crucial for preparing students for the complexities of the modern workforce. By creating a dynamic and engaging environment, ConCourse enables students to expand their professional networks, gain firsthand knowledge about their desired career paths and receive invaluable guidance on navigating their professional journeys. In the academic year 2023, ConCourse took great strides in enhancing the learning experience for students by hosting a diverse array of sessions covering a wide spectrum of topics. The year was kickstarted with the much-anticipated ‘Welcoming the FYs’ series, designed with the specific purpose of providing freshers with a warm and informative introduction to college life. This initiative was born out of the recognition that the transition to college can be overwhelming and by offering insights and advice from beloved alumni like Mr. Craig Travasso and Mr. Tarang Gupta, we aimed to ease this transition. 35

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 36 Content: Their personal journeys served as relatable examples for the students, demonstrating how to navigate the challenges and opportunities that lie ahead in their college experience. These sessions have become a perennial favorite among students, setting a positive and motivational tone for the year. Building on the momentum of the ‘Welcoming the FYs’ series, ConCourse collaborated with the department's flagship financial fest, Entourage, to host a powerful session with renowned investigative journalist Mr. Sanjay Singh. Known for his groundbreaking work in exposing the Abdul Telgi scam, Mr. Singh’s session was carefully selected to provide students with a rare and valuable glimpse into the world of investigative journalism. The purpose of this session was not only to captivate the audience with its gripping narrative but also to inspire students to consider the ethical and societal implications of their future careers. By highlighting the critical role that media plays in shaping public opinion and holding power to account, we aimed to encourage students to think critically about the impact they can have on society. Another standout session of the year was led by Mr. Hitesh Parmar, a highly respected visiting faculty member whose engaging teaching style and deep knowledge of the subject matter made a lasting impression on the students. The decision to invite Mr. Parmar was based on his ability to make complex topics accessible and engaging, thereby ensuring that students not only understand the material but also appreciate its relevance to their future careers. In addition to these highlighted sessions, ConCourse hosted a series of discussions that explored a broad range of topics, reflecting the diverse interests and aspirations of our student body. These discussions were carefully chosen to address current trends and issues that are shaping the future of work. For instance, a deep dive into the rising influence of Artificial Intelligence was organized to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to navigate an increasingly automated world. An exploration of the Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) course was included to provide clarity on a key pathway to a successful career in finance, while an in-depth analysis of the geopolitical ramifications of the ongoing Russia- Ukraine War was designed to provoke thoughtful discussion and critical thinking among students. Each of these sessions was intended to not only inform but also to challenge students to think critically and engage with complex global issues. Beyond the confines of the classroom, ConCourse expanded its reach 36

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 37 Content: by organizing visits and collaborative events that provided students with practical, real-world experiences. One such event was a visit to the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), where students attended an exclusive session with the Association of National Exchanges Members of India (ANMI). This visit was strategically planned to offer students a firsthand look at the workings of financial markets, bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application. By witnessing the operations of one of the world’s leading stock exchanges, students were able to gain valuable insights into the dynamics of financial markets, enhancing their understanding of the subject matter. Furthermore, ConCourse collaborated with the Observer Research Foundation (ORF), a leading think tank, to offer students the unique opportunity to attend the ORF Spark Dialogue 2024 at the TISS Convention Center. This prestigious event brought together thought leaders, policymakers and academics to discuss pressing global issues. The decision to collaborate with ORF was driven by our commitment to providing students with a platform to engage with experts and expand their understanding of international relations and geopolitics. The experience was both intellectually stimulating and professionally enriching, underscoring the importance of interdisciplinary learning in today’s interconnected world. By exposing students to such high-caliber discussions, we aimed to broaden their perspectives and encourage them to think critically about global challenges. ConCourse is more than just a committee; it is a journey of enlightenment, empowerment and connection. Our mission is rooted in the belief that education extends beyond the classroom, encompassing experiences that prepare students not only for academic success but also for the challenges and opportunities of the real world. As we look forward to the future, we are excited about the possibilities that lie ahead for ConCourse. We are committed to reaching even greater heights, continuing to shape the minds, aspirations and careers of students. Our vision is to ensure that every student who participates in ConCourse leaves with a richer understanding of the world, a stronger professional network, and the confidence to pursue their dreams with passion and purpose. The foundation we have laid will serve as a stepping stone for future successes, as we strive to create an even more impactful and enriching experience for our students. 37

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 38 Content: ENDEAVOUR 2023 Faculty Advisor: Prof. Yasmin Singaporewala Student Members: Nandini Karani, Saahib Gera, Krish Tanwani, Zoya Lala, Khushi Shah, Aaditya Khare, Nishka Dixit Endeavour, established in 2018, has quickly grown into a reputable Editorial Club that plays a vital role within the BAF, BBI and BFM departments of Jai Hind College. The club is dedicated to fostering a range of communication and critical thinking skills, offering students an opportunity to engage in a holistic and multidisciplinary environment. It provides a platform for students to develop talents that not only enhance their academic journeys but also prepare them for real-world challenges. Over the years, Endeavour has expanded its activities and its vision, establishing itself as a pillar of intellectual and creative growth for students across these departments. The mission of Endeavour is clear: to create a space where students can refine their skills through meaningful projects that stretch beyond the traditional classroom environment. By offering a diverse array of teams and initiatives, the club enables its members to delve deeper into their passions, whether it be writing, research, social media or alumni relations. The club’s structure is designed to nurture talent, encourage intellectual curiosity, and provide students with the tools they need to succeed in both their academic and professional lives. One of the key teams within Endeavour is the Blog Team, where students have the chance to sharpen their writing abilities and experiment with different genres. This team contributes to the club’s WordPress blog, covering an eclectic range of topics. Recent blog posts have ranged from discussing the ‘History of the World According to Bread’ to delving into more personal and introspective topics like ‘Finding Beauty in the Art of Letting Go.’ These pieces exemplify the diversity of thought and creativity that the Blog Team fosters. By encouraging students to write about topics that interest them, the club allows its members to develop their voice and writing style, which will serve them well in future academic or professional writing tasks. The Research Club is another cornerstone of Endeavour, providing students with opportunities to engage in critical discussions and conduct research on a wide variety of topics. The focus of the Research Club is to cultivate analytical thinking, enabling students to develop their research skills in a practical, hands-on way. 38

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 39 Content: Some of the club’s recent research projects have been particularly innovative and relevant to today’s fast-evolving world. For example, studies on “ChatGPT's Impact on the Higher Education System in Mumbai” and “The Influence of Unified Payment Interface (UPI) on Student Lifestyles in Big Cities” reflect the club’s commitment to exploring topics that are current and impactful. Additionally, the research project on “Marketing Strategies in the Digital Age, Specifically Leveraging Social Media” demonstrates the club's focus on understanding modern business and marketing trends, providing valuable insights for students interested in careers in finance, business, or marketing. These research endeavors offer students a deeper understanding of current economic, technological and societal trends, giving them a competitive edge as they prepare for their future careers. Participation in competitions, such as those at Pillai College of Arts Commerce and Science and Laxmichand Golwala College of Commerce, allows students to showcase their research and analytical skills while gaining exposure to real-world academic challenges. These events are crucial for fostering intellectual growth, as they encourage students to apply their learning in competitive environments and sharpen their critical thinking abilities. The Alumni Team plays a pivotal role in connecting current students with the distinguished alumni of Jai Hind College. By facilitating insightful discussions and fostering lasting professional relationships, the team helps students gain access to invaluable career advice, industry insights, and networking opportunities. Alumni can provide mentorship, share their professional journeys and offer guidance that can help students navigate their own career paths. One of the recent highlights was a session with Zain Al Hussain Naqvi, an alumnus who spoke on how to crack the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) exams. Sessions like these are designed to give students practical, actionable advice, providing them with the tools and knowledge they need to succeed in competitive professional environments. Alumni engagement has always been crucial to the mission of Endeavour, as it creates a bridge between academia and industry, ensuring that students are prepared for the challenges that lie ahead. The Social Media Team within Endeavour has taken on the critical role of driving online engagement and expanding the club’s presence across various platforms. 39

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 40 Content: In today’s digital age, having a strong online presence is essential and Endeavour’s social media team works tirelessly to create compelling content that promotes the club’s events, achievements, and initiatives. The team focuses on creating dynamic posts that resonate with their audience, using a combination of graphics, storytelling and timely updates to keep students engaged. By expanding the club’s presence on platforms such as Instagram ,the Social Media Team ensures that Endeavour remains relevant in the digital space, attracting new members and maintaining its reputation as a hub for intellectual and creative exploration. A notable achievement of Endeavour in 2023 was the rebranding of the club’s logo. This redesign reflects the club’s evolving identity and commitment to innovation. The updated logo was created to better align with the club's vision of modernity, creativity and professionalism, and it serves as a visual representation of the progress that Endeavour has made over the years. The rebranding also signals the club's readiness to embrace change while staying true to its core mission of nurturing student talent. Endeavour’s contributions are not limited to internal projects. These activities showcase the students' ability to apply their learning in real- world situations, thereby solidifying their reputation as academically competent and versatile individuals. Such external engagements also provide students with opportunities to network with peers from other institutions, further broadening their professional circles. In summary, Endeavour has made significant strides in creating an environment where students can grow intellectually, creatively, and professionally. The club’s diverse range of teams and projects ensures that every student can find a place where their talents are nurtured and their ambitions are supported. From conducting cutting-edge research and creating engaging blogs to fostering strong alumni connections and enhancing the club’s digital footprint, Endeavour is a testament to Jai Hind College’s commitment to holistic education. The club’s future is bright, with plans to continue expanding its activities, hosting more events and providing students with the tools they need to succeed in both their academic and professional pursuits. As Endeavour moves forward, it remains dedicated to its mission of shaping the minds and careers of future generations. 40

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 41 Content: Entourage, the flagship financial fest of Jai Hind College, organized by the departments of BAF, BBI and BFM, was meticulously crafted to redefine the perception of finance as a dry and monotonous field. The fest aimed to provide a dynamic platform where students could explore, learn, and compete in the world of finance, fostering a vibrant exchange of ideas and attracting participants from various colleges. The primary objective was not only to educate but also to inspire students by providing them with opportunities to network with industry experts and renowned figures from the finance world. The journey of Entourage 2023 began with the strategic election of the core committee. This team, comprising 26 members from six different departments, was carefully selected based on their skills, areas of expertise, and a shared vision of making Entourage 2023 an unparalleled success. Preparation for Entourage 2023 went beyond the logistical aspects of event management. The team emphasized the importance of community engagement and real-world learning experiences. This approach was exemplified by the team’s visit to Door Step School to celebrate Independence Day with the students. This activity was not just a gesture of goodwill but a deliberate effort to instill a sense of social responsibility among the team members and participants. The visit underscored the idea that finance professionals should be aware of and contribute to the broader community, reflecting the fest’s ethos that true success in finance involves giving back to society. To further enrich the participants' experience, Entourage 2023 included a series of educational sessions aimed at providing deep insights into the financial world. One such session was conducted by Mr. Sanjay Singh, who discussed the Abdul Telgi scam. This session was strategically included to align with the fest’s goal of promoting financial ethics and integrity. By exposing students to real-world examples of financial malpractice, the fest aimed to underscore the importance of ethical behavior in finance, preparing students to navigate the industry with a strong moral compass. ENTOURAGE 2023 Coordinator: Prof. Yasmin Singaporewala. Core Committee:Shreya Shah (Chairperson), Jaithra Sood (Vice Chairperson), Pranav (Vice Chairperson), Tanishka Puntambekar (Vice Chairperson), Shikhar Jain (Vice Chairperson), Prachi Aidasani (Vice Chairperson) 41

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 42 Content: Amidst the rigorous preparations, the Entourage Premier League (EPL) was introduced as the first pre-event. This cricket tournament, where teams were owned by the core committee, served multiple purposes. It provided a much-needed break from the intense planning process, allowing team members to relax and bond in a fun, informal setting. Additionally, it fostered a sense of camaraderie and team spirit, which were essential for maintaining high morale and ensuring smooth collaboration among the organizing committee members. The EPL was not just a game but a strategic tool to build a cohesive team environment, which was crucial for the successful execution of the fest. The grand reveal of the ‘Finarcade’ theme was a pivotal moment in the fest’s preparations. The theme, an innovative blend of finance and arcade, was chosen to make the subject of finance more accessible and engaging for students. By integrating gaming elements with financial challenges, the fest aimed to capture the interest of participants and demonstrate that finance can be both exciting and intellectually stimulating. This theme was also intended to challenge participants to think creatively and approach financial problems from a fresh, innovative perspective.On the 12th of December 2023, the team visited the Access Life Assistance Foundation, where they spent time with children battling cancer. This visit was a crucial part of Entourage’s commitment to holistic development. This experience was designed to instill a sense of compassion and responsibility in the participants, encouraging them to use their financial skills for the greater good. As the main event days approached, the excitement was palpable. The committee organized ‘Double Trouble Day,’ a pre-event where students dressed as iconic pairs. This event was not only a fun way to build anticipation for the fest but also a strategic move to encourage creativity and team spirit among participants. The light- hearted nature of the event helped to alleviate stress and foster a positive, energetic atmosphere in the lead-up to the main event. When the main event days finally arrived, the venue was transformed into a vibrant representation of the Finarcade theme. This transformation was carefully planned to create an immersive experience for attendees, transporting them into an arcade-like atmosphere where finance was the central focus. The events, including Wall Street Saga, Market Mayhem, and Portfolio Palooza, were designed not just to test participants' financial knowledge but 42

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 43 Content: also to enhance their critical thinking, teamwork, and real-world application skills. Each event was crafted with the intention of providing participants with a comprehensive learning experience that would prepare them for future challenges in the financial world. The fest culminated in the flagship event, Mr. and Ms. Entourage, followed by an emotional closing ceremony. The success of Entourage 2023 was a testament to the dedication and hard work of the 214 members who had devoted five months to its preparation. This event not only achieved its goal of redefining finance for students but also forged lasting friendships, built strong networks, and created memories that will be cherished forever. Looking ahead, the entire team is motivated to take Entourage to even greater heights next year, armed with the experiences and lessons learned from this unforgettable journey. The foundation laid by Entourage 2023 will serve as a stepping stone for future successes. 43

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 44 Content: TALAASH 2023-24 Faculty Advisor: Dr Rakhi Sharma Student Secretary: Aadit Agarwal Team Talash Organizing Committee: Aashna Doctor (Events), Meghashree Somani (Legal & Compliance), Saptami Somaiya (Creatives & Media), Nakuul Shahani (Public Relations), Tanya Bhambani (Finance), Shariya Khan (Executions), Jeremiah Bobbetti (Marketing) Jai Hind College (Empowered Autonomous) has long been recognized for nurturing creative, adaptive, and innovative learning among its students. As a proud part of this institution, TALAASH stands as a distinguished legacy of the BMS and BBA departments. Spanning over 24 remarkable years, this iconic event has earned its place as one of the oldest and most celebrated festivals within Jai Hind College and across Mumbai. TALAASH offers numerous opportunities for students to learn, grow, and showcase their talents through a diverse range of engaging events such as the Treasure Hunt and the Fashion Show. The 24th edition of TALAASH was a testament to tradition and innovation. The two-day cultural extravaganza, held on January 27 and 29, 2024, highlighted the dedication and creativity of the Management Department and the student organizing team. Day 1: Management Meets Creativity The first day of TALAASH was packed with intellectually stimulating management games that challenged participants to think quickly and strategically. Events like ‘Yin to My Yang’ and ‘IQ-EQ’ tested participants’ skills in creative problem-solving and critical thinking. The day wasn’t limited to managerial challenges; it also featured enthralling cultural performances that captivated the audience. ‘Dramastra’ and ‘Shock On!’ showcased the incredible talent of students, judged by a star-studded panel including Mr. Namit Shah, Ms. Inayat Sood, and Mr. Aadesh Shah, among others. Day 2: A Grand Cultural Finale The second day marked the grand finale of TALAASH and was dedicated entirely to cultural performances, providing a platform for participants to shine and express themselves creatively. The day began with a wildly entertaining ‘Just A Minute (JAM)’ session, hosted by the charismatic Hormuz Ragina, affectionately known as “Jam Master HoHo.” His infectious energy had the audience thoroughly engaged and entertained. 44

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 45 Content: Following this, the ‘Mr. and Ms. Regalia’ pageant allowed participants to showcase their unique talents and personalities. The day continued with ‘Plot Twist,’ a high-energy dance competition judged by industry experts Shehzaan Khan, Sachin Sharma, and Shraddha Tiwari. Colleges from all over Mumbai brought their best performances, dazzling the audience with their creativity and dedication. The finale of the day featured the much-anticipated fashion show, ‘Quill & Couture,’ where participating colleges presented captivating runway displays, leaving a lasting impression on everyone in attendance. No TALAASH would be complete without its flagship event—the Treasure Hunt. Spanning a vast area across Mumbai, this exhilarating challenge put participants through a whirlwind of emotions as they raced against time to solve intricate clues crafted by the organizing team. A Collective Triumph The seamless execution of TALAASH 2024 was a testament to the hard work, passion, and meticulous planning of the student team, led by Student Secretary Aadit Aggarwal. Working alongside him were the dedicated Student Joint Secretaries. Their collaborative efforts, combined with the support of the faculty, ensured that TALAASH 2024 was an unforgettable experience for all participants and attendees. The event truly celebrated creativity, resilience, and the enduring spirit of Jai Hind College. TEDx AT JAI HIND COLLEGE Faculty Advisor: Dr Rakhi Sharma Student Facilitators: Jai Melwani, Maanav Chugani, Tanya Thapar and Mahek Jain 45

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 46 Content: On December 9, 2023, TEDx Jai Hind College made a grand return, bringing together a compelling line up of 10 speakers, 2 performers, and an alumni host. The highly anticipated event centered around the resonant theme of (r)evolution, capturing stories of change, resilience, and transformation from diverse fields. The program commenced with opening remarks by Principal Dr. Vijay Dabholkar, followed by Dr. Rajesh Thadani, President of the Board, who spoke passionately about the ongoing social shifts aligned with the day’s theme. Session 1: Transformative Journeys The first session kicked off at 10:30 AM, captivating both students and guests with a memorable roster of speakers who shared stories of power, resilience, creativity, and triumph. The session began with Hon. Judge Shelyna V. Brown, recipient of the 100 Black Women of Silicon Valley’s Excellence in Advocacy Award. Judge Brown explored themes of power and liberation that transcended the confines of the courtroom, offering a deeply moving narrative on justice and advocacy. Next, the stage was graced by Amar Mangrulkar, a self-taught musical prodigy who, at 30, left the corporate grind to pursue his passion for music. His compositions, which have found their way into TV ads, Netflix series, and hit movies, have earned him three Abby Awards and a Filmfare nomination. Mangrulkar’s journey showcased the transformative power of following one’s dreams. Dr. Ravi Lalwani, a global martial arts maestro and recipient of the prestigious Bharat Gaurav Puruskar, followed with a gripping narrative. Having been knighted by the Government of Italy, his story underscored the discipline, commitment, and impact of martial arts. Lalwani’s insights were as impactful as his judo techniques, inspiring attendees to channel strength from within. The first session concluded with Pralav Dhyani, whose harrowing experience surviving a pirate hijacking on a voyage from Seychelles Island to Zanzibar gave way to a successful career as a founder of a shipping firm in the UAE. His story of determination and courage deeply resonated with the audience, demonstrating the ability to overcome life’s fiercest storms. Session 2: Inspiring Perspectives The second session resumed at 1:30 PM with Hormuz Ragina, a celebrated cultural figure and “JAM Master.” Known for his contagious enthusiasm and light hearted storytelling, Ragina had the audience 46

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 47 Content: laughing and energized, bringing joy and a fresh spirit to the event. Next up was Dar Gai, a Ukrainian filmmaker, screenwriter, and director known for her evocative music videos. Gai spoke about the intense struggles and doubts faced by artists, sharing personal anecdotes that resonated deeply with the creative minds in attendance. Her heartfelt talk offered a raw and candid view of the artistic journey, leaving a lasting impact. The session continued with Hitesh Matlani, famously known as Paco de la India. A visionary in cryptocurrency, Matlani detailed his ‘Run with Bitcoin’ adventure, an audacious journey spanning 40 countries in 400 days, funded entirely by Bitcoin. His tales of challenges and triumphs captivated the audience, illustrating the potential of decentralized finance. Dr. Sheetal Sabharwal, a renowned gynaecologist, concluded Session 2 with a powerful presentation on her groundbreaking ‘Water Birth’ campaign of 2003. Dr. Sabharwal passionately highlighted the benefits of water birth and discussed innovative perspectives on female reproductive health, sparking meaningful dialogue among attendees. Concluding Talks Hussein Dalal, a celebrated Bollywood actor, director, and dialogue writer, took the stage to exemplify the core ethos of TEDx Jai Hind College: hope. Dalal shared his personal and professional journey, inspiring the audience with reflections on perseverance and creativity within the entertainment industry. The event concluded with Parul Gulati, a successful entrepreneur and social media influencer. Gulati’s motivational talk resonated with students, emphasizing self-belief, resilience, and the pursuit of dreams. Her words offered a fitting close to a day filled with inspiration and insight. Curtain Call The event ended with a heartfelt curtain call led by host Shashwat Mathur, who expressed gratitude to the speakers, performers, organizers, and attendees for making TEDx Jai Hind College 2023 a resounding success. STUDENT INDUCTION PROGRAMME - DEEKSHARAMBH Faculty Coordinators: Dr Anamika Purohit, Ms Firdous Sarnoubat, Ms Radhika Rajwani 47

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 48 Content: The Student Induction Programme - Deeksharambh (2023-24) started with a bang this year with its first offline session - Orientation - in the Auditorium of Jai Hind College. The First Year students from different streams came in big numbers to get initiated into this unique induction programme specially curated for them at Jai Hind College, Autonomous, by Faculty Coordinators - Dr. Anamika Purohit; Ms Firdous Sarnoubat; and Ms Radhika Rajwani under the guidance of Dr. Sreela Dasgupta (Dean - Academic Affairs) and Principal Sir, Prof. (Dr.) Vijay Dabholkar. The Orientation was a means to initiate the FY students into the spirit of Jai Hind College, helping them recognise the rationale of an induction programme that was meant to ensure a smooth transition from school- life to college-life. This session facilitated a semi-formal interaction of the FY students with their seniors in the Second and Third Year as well as Alumni who especially came on board to share their college-life experiences with the young students. Dr. Dabholkar and Dr. Dasgupta shared their insights with the students too. The second offline session that was on the concept of Self and was convened by Ms Mahek Punjabi (the college-counsellor), who helped students explore a plethora of concerns related to self - such as, identity construction, body-image issues, loneliness, peer-pressure, need to inculcate self-love etc. This was followed by the third session, which was online, on Family and Society conducted by the three Faculty Coordinators, Dr. Purohit, Ms Sarnoubat, and Ms Rajwani, and was all about exploring the next two concentric circles in the course of self- development. Evolution of familial relationships; need for bonding; expanding the community-circle; individual contribution; harmonious coexistence were some of the concerns discussed. The subsequent session was on concepts of Nature and World conducted online by Dr. Devangi Chanchad and Ms N.K. Jyothi respectively. The session explored the importance of conserving our environment, taking responsibility as youngsters to preserve nature, avoid wastage, be inclusive and open to change, explore self in connection with the world. This was followed by a Concluding Session, which was about tying the strings of all the sessions as well as help the students perceive their college life experiences creatively. Students were asked to send in their creative expressions on college-life or any of the SIP topics, and the response overwhelmed us. The students were finally asked to submit their reflective journal for SIP and provide valuable feedback to us. 48

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 49 Content: A special thanks to the Student Council and our dedicated team of Student Volunteers, who’ve been an important source of support for all of us in this endeavour. XPLORE: FUN WITH SCIENCE ON NATIONAL SCIENCE DAY Chairperson: Niloufer K. Kotwal Committee Members: Dr. Shuchita Deepak, Dr. Kruti Pandya, Dr. Manisha Deshpande, Dr. Devangi Chachad, Ms. Khatija Atthar, Dr. Onkar Lotlikar, Mr Pranil Waiker, Ms Shraddhadevi Singh ‘Xplore’ is an Annual Intra-collegiate Science Exhibition held each year to commemorate National Science Day which falls on 28th February and celebrates the occasion of Sir C.V. Raman’s Birth Anniversary. It encourages young, bright and innovative students to hone their research ideas and skills. This event helps in inculcating scientific temperament among students. Some students make models, posters to explain a scientific theme/concept of their choice, while others are involved in multidisciplinary research project, literature review and exhibit their work in the form of either poster presentation or oral presentation. While this exhibition brings out the creativity in them, it also promotes the culture of pure science. By participating in this event students get an exposure to not only research but also to other soft skills like presentation, managing and teamwork. Xplore is an endeavour to make students think outside the box and go beyond their curriculum. The main objective of ‘Xplore’ is to spread the joy of discovering facts while doing science, and have fun at the same time. Through this exhibition we try to inculcate into young minds love for science. Since Xplore caters to all undergraduate students, the objectives change as per their level. First Year students – They select a topic/concept which interests them and prepare charts or models to explain the same. Main aim is to make them read beyond the syllabus and to apply the theoretical knowledge learnt throughout the Academic year. Second Year students – They select a topic/concept which interests them and prepare charts or models to explain the same. However, as compared to the First-Year level, the Second-year students are expected to do review literature. Main objective is to improve their presentation skills, learn to review and apply literature, and increase their understanding of a particular topic of interest thus building stronger concepts. 49

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 50 Content: Third Year students – At the Third-Year level, students are expected to identify a problem and attempt to find a solution for the same. The main aim is to inculcate a research aptitude, that includes reading and analysing research papers from various peer reviewed journals, and applying the techniques reviewed in literature in finding a solution to the problem identified. By participating in Xplore, our students realise that even seemingly abstract concepts can have real life applications. Students develop curiosity and are self-motivated. As team-work is encouraged and demonstration of their work is effectively communicated, they also develop problem solving skills, improved presentation skills and soft skills. The Xplore Exhibition is open to students of other institutions, and our participating students explain various concepts of science in a lucid language to school children. Our students ensure that “Science is Learning with fun” for school children and teachers from non- science backgrounds, thus helping promote the underlying beauty of sciences. This year Xplore had an overall participation of about 150 Teams with a total of over 300 participants. We are eternally grateful for the support we have received year after year from Teachers and Students alike, and we hope to expand Xplore each year to make students not only have “Fun with Science”, but help in their personality development and hone their soft-skills. 50

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 77 Content: SO MOVED...AND YET, SO STATIONERY ~ SIYA NARWEKAR FYBA Pencil. Pencil was otherworldly, Pencil was ethereal, Pencil was a dream, Pencil was his dream. Eraser gaped in wonder - what with her shiny, pointed head and her long, striped sundress - Pencil was incomparable, a goddess in disguise. And today was the day, the day he would ‘rub off’ the right and smart way. Today he won’t don Apsara or even Camlin; today was the day for Staedtler. Oh, how she’d sharpen her edges and dance across the notebook pages on their first date today! He hadn’t known her for long, perhaps just a week now. They’d met at work - this haunted house the giants in suits and ties around them seemed to call ‘Bright Paper Publishing House’. It most certainly wasn’t a place for the lily-livered. They were both severely overworked. It was sheer terror - they would demand her to run and dance across the pages, sometimes for hours on end. It had started with timid glances across the ‘editor’s’ desk - hesitant, doubting, wanting; and yet so deliriously passionate that he ached all over with desire. And yet, they’d never dared to trespass the chaste line of formal etiquette into the blazing realm of impulse-driven longing. They’d never dared to, they’d never dared to; until that one fateful night when unbridled carnality seized their rationale. The editor’s auto ride home was choppy and chaotic; and veiled in the shadowy darkness of her handbag, concealed from the probing eyes of light, they had shuffled close; their surfaces grazing, but barely so; brushing, but barely so; touching, but barely so. It had been too much to shoulder. 77

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 78 Content: There had been too much rubbing and too much friction - and SO MUCH heat. Driven by desire, overtaken by impulse, he had grabbed hold of her slender curve - and sparks had flown; as had wooden shavings and rubber dust.... The Rise of Entrepreneurial Spirit in Indian Women Rabiya Saudagar, G.N. Khalsa College First Prize Winner "What? There's a woman behind this entire business? You've got to be kidding!" That's a cringe-worthy response you're likely to hear from an archetypal Indian. Women owning and running businesses is not an entirely alien phenomenon, since we’re all aware of the tiny cottage industries we learnt about in school. But that’s that. For a long time, women have had to confine themselves to petty businesses. But why have women not been as dominant as their male counterparts in business? Well, for starters, here are some of the most common problems women face in India: The all-too-familiar red tape. • Skeptical bankers denying credit and questioning credibility despite the proof that their loan repayment rates are higher than that of men. (United Nations Industrial Development Organization) 78

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 79 Content: • Competition from the organized sector & the male-dominant entrepreneurial India • Family ties and homemaking responsibilities • Illiteracy and the lack of sound education • A patriarchal society that doesn’t willingly support or encourage women in business • A lack of the entrepreneurial spirit and the drive to take big risks • A so-called lack of self-confidence This is India – the land of ‘rivaaz’ (tradition). As a country, we had been lagging behind on the development front in terms of 'women power' – and quite understandably so, given the orthodoxy and prevailing attitude towards the weaker sex, especially when it came to the 'role' of a woman in society. The very fact that we still read terms like 'the weaker sex' shows that this nauseating bias is ingrained in our patriarchal minds. The winds of change have finally arrived, albeit a little late. Better late than never, right? Welcome to modern-day India – a growing hub of opportunities waiting to be grabbed, a diverse mishmash of industries in the metropolitans, an open stage for all genders to take control of, and a marginally more open-minded community that welcomes change (for good). It has been (and still is), however, a struggle for some women to break the shackles to aim big, take the plunge, and realize their dreams. Some get lost in oblivion, some are held back by fear and pressure, some lack conviction, some do not get the support they need, and then there are some who, despite all of the above, make it through to the top. 79

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 80 Content: Here's looking at some of the most interesting and awe- inspiring stories of women who made a choice, stuck to it, and set an example for us to follow: 1. The 'compassionate capitalist' who did not build Biocon overnight Corporate-Secretary, Dr. Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw CMD, Biocon Ltd. Yes, she is the chairperson & MD of Biocon Ltd. (the stellar biotechnology company), the current chairperson of IIM- Bangalore, and one of Forbes’ World’s 100 Most Powerful Women. But life wasn’t easy for her when she forayed into the industry. With the degree and some experience as a trainee in Aussie- land, she came back to India to explore opportunities for growth. But to her dismay, she was told off here because apparently “being a Master Brewer is a man’s thing.” With solid educational credentials behind her, she met Leslie Auchincloss, who was incidentally looking for an entrepreneur to help set up an Indian subsidiary of his company, Biocon Bio chemicals Ltd. Her loan application was turned down thrice by Indian banks. Biocon India was finally born in 1978 in the garage of Kiran’s rented house in Bangalore with a seed capital of rs. 10,000. From heading a company that started in a tiny garage to become India's largest publicly traded Biopharmaceutical Company (with $460 billion in revenue in 2014) that dispenses products in 85 countries, it’s been one hell of a rollercoaster ride for Kiran. 2. The multitasking, multitalented serial entrepreneur, Corporate-Secretary, Sairee Chahal, Founder & CEO, Sheroes.in & Fleximoms As the founder and CEO of Sheroes.in and Fleximoms, Sairee Chahal has crafted two businesses based on the struggles she 80

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 81 Content: had to deal with, being a family-oriented woman in India, thus empowering Indian women to find excellent and flexible work opportunities and enabling businesses to find the right female candidates. Sheroes was launched in 2014, and has already witnessed a footfall of 6,000 companies and over half a million women across 5,000 locations. The need for establishing a career shop like Fleximom arose from the fact that things had gotten difficult at the workplace for Sairee when she became a mother, thanks largely to the inflexible work formats. Disinclined towards giving up what would’ve been a highly successful corporate career, Sairee found another way out – a better way out. The two companies that she heads are meant for women like Sairee – women like you – who wish to strike the perfect work- life balance. 3. Breaking the social stigma with comics, Corporate- Secretary, Aditi Gupta, Founder, Menstrupedia The two companies that she heads are meant for women like Sairee – women like you – who wish to strike the perfect work- life balance. Amongst the most inspirational social entrepreneurs out there, Aditi Gupta is easily the gutsiest. With an aim to depict the concept of menstruation in a positive light, Aditi founded Menstrupedia in 2012, and has already managed to garner an average of 2 lac visits per month. The idea behind the business stemmed from her own alarming experience as a child, when she got her first period at the age of 12 (and was asked to keep mum about it). A subject that is considered taboo in our society, ‘menstruation’ got a delightful makeover when Aditi and her husband Tuhin created the online database of comic books that aim to educate young girls about the natural human phenomenon. 81

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 82 Content: Aditi’s struggles and inhuman experiences as a child have translated into a social cause that will educate millions of people. Touching story, that. 4. Making the world a better place with data, Corporate- Secretary, Prukalpa Sankar, Co-founder, SocialCops The gifted 10-year-old Prukalpa coded an HTML website for Harry Potter fans and could have gone on to become whatever she wanted. But she chose to do something for the betterment of the society. Enter SocialCops, a social enterprise that uses technology to harness the voice of the citizens to help make synergistic data-driven decisions in health, education and infrastructure. With a dwindling bank balance of $5000, there were problems galore, in spite of promises to fund the startup initially. But SocialCops managed to raise $320,000 in the first round of funding and Prukalpa hasn’t looked back ever since 5. Two good to be true, Corporate-Secretary, Aardra Chandra Mouli and Gayathri Thankachi V. , Founders, Aeka Biochemicals Pvt. Ltd. Establishing a biotech company in Kerela is no child’s play. Ask the owners of Aeka Biochemicals Pvt. Ltd. – the first biotech and biochemical startup venture based out of Trivandrum in Kerala wholly owned by women – and they will tell you the range of challenges they came face-to-face with. From the usual red tape to lack of information availability, and from complex legal processes to uncooperative officials, Aardra and Gayatri have a whole bunch of appalling-yet-funny anecdotes to share. Aimed at putting the environment first and meeting the needs of the local community, Aeka was born in 2014 and is growing from strength-to-strength. 82

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 83 Content: From technology to finance, and from art to retail, players like Anisha Singh, Purnima Rao, Suruchi Wagh, Surabhi Dewra, Rashi Choudhary, Ankita Gaba, Ashwini Asokan and Rashi Narang are just some examples of the hordes of startup owners who are beseeching other women to stand up and do what they believe in. They fought all odds to compete with the prevailing male dominance in the business world. All it took was a little bit of courage, belief and the readiness to go all-guns-blazing. Entrepreneurship has become a new wave among women in recent years. Women have become aware of their existence their rights and their work situation. However, women of the middle class are not too eager to alter their role in fear of social backlash. Highly educated technically sound and professionally qualified women should be encouraged for managing their own business rather than dependent on wage employment outlets. The unexplored talents of young women can be identified, trained and used for various types of industries to increase productivity in the industrial sector. A desirable environment is necessary for every woman to inculcate entrepreneurial values and involve greatly in business dealing. In this manner, more women should be encouraged for to take up entrepreneurial ventures. 83

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 91 Content: DEPARTMENT AND ASSOCIATION/ SOCIETY/FORUM REPORTS Head of Department: Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani Members: CA. Jimy Wankadia, Dr. Fatema Fanuswala, Prachi Mane, Pratishtha Sharma Student Secretary: Khushi Shah and Raksha Ravichandran Student Members: Khushi Shah, Raksha Ravichandran, Mithilesh Shahani, Iqra Ahmed Vraddhee Khatter, Riya Madan, Farzaan Pachha, Saharsh Singhal, Sukriti Sen, Kaustubh Mane, Saumya Kessar, Alshifa Khan The Accountancy Department was set up in 1980 and is engaged in Accountancy Subjects of B.Com.,M.Com. and B.A. Courses. The main objective is to make the students knowledgeable and skilled to improve their employability and making them professionally competent locally, as well. Our department profile is as follows:- 1) Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani (HOD, Accountancy, Member of E-Cell, Accountancy Association, IIC, Sindhi Circle, BOS member of the JHC Accountancy Department) 2) C.A. Jimy Wankadia (Patron & Member of Accountancy Association, BOS member of the Accountancy Department, Chairman of Finance Committee,Sind Educationists’ Association, Parent Trust) 3) Dr.Fatema Fanuswala (Member of Accountancy Association, CSR Committee, BOS member of the JHC Accountancy Department,V.C. Nominee of B.Com/B.B.I of KBP College) 4) Prachi Mane (Member of Accountancy Association, CSR Committee, BOS member of the JHC Accountancy Department, NAAC Criterion III) 5) Pratishtha Sharma (Member of Accountancy Association, CSR Committee, NAAC Criterion II, BOS member of St. Mira’s College, Pune & Jai Hind College Accountancy) Being first year of NEP we have strive our level best in framing and setting up the curriculum of our courses. Our Department had its BOS for A.Y. 23-24 on February 6, 2023 to discuss and approve subjects as per NEP pattern. At present we offered subjects like Taxation, Financial Accountancy, Cost Accountancy, Management Accountancy and the like at the UG level where Accounts is a Major Subject (4 credits). Under NEP, we also offered one Accountancy subject to B.A.; B.Sc students as their “Open Elective”. We organised many seminars in areas of Accountancy & Finance. One of which is on “Career opportunities in FinX” for UG and PG students. In order to foster Accounting culture amongst students we have one “Accountancy Association” which conducts 4 events primarily related to Accounts. In the Academic Year 23-24 we have conducted following 4 events: 1) Personal Financial Planning on August 1, 2024 2) Chat GPT and uses of AI in Business Guidance on ACCA on September 15, 2024 3) Elevation on December 24, 2024 4) Budget Analysis on February 3, 2024 Jai Hind College report Accountancy 91

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 92 Content: Our department is enriched by one more Ph.D this year i.e. Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani got awarded with degree in February, 2024. This year we have 2 research papers published by Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani ‘Adoption of Digital Payment in the wholesale market of South Mumbai’ and Prachi Mane’s ‘A study on evaluating role of lead bank scheme in financial inclusion of Rural Areas’. Additionally, Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani and Ms. Pratishtha Sharma have co-authored a book titled ‘Financial Accountancy for Proprietorship’ published by Sheth Publishers Pvt. Ltd. We have mentored 32 students of PG for Research Project. Ms. Reshma guided one team for the Avishkar Research Convention. Our aim is to promote Accounting & research culture amongst students and faculties through our teachings and activities. We aim to start our own “Accountancy Library” for Commerce students so as to elevate their learning process. We continuously strive to work & develop academically which will benefit students and the institution at large; therefore we have attended following seminars/FDP: Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani 1) Invited as a Resource person for the workshop on Generation of Ideas by L.D. Sonavane College, Kalyan and Bhavans College, Chowpatty. 2) Invited as a peer team member for the Academic Audit of Tolani College of Commerce,Andheri in May 2024 3) Attended 2 days workshop on Implementation of NEP organised by Sathaye College, in March 2024. Prachi Mane 1)Attended “JHC-NEP Implementation Series Talks” conducted by Jai Hind College in August, 2024 2) Attended an FDP on “Research Tools and Techniques with Reference to Chat GPT” organized by Mohindar Kabal Singh Degree College of Arts and Commerce in August, 2023 3)Research Workshop conducted by Bharatiya Vidya Bhavan Somani College in August, 2023 4) Attended online webinar on “ Tips on publishing Research Papers in Reputed Journals” in September,2023 Pratishtha Sharma 1) Attended FDP on “Research Tools and Techniques with Reference to Chat GPT” organized by Mohindar Kabal Singh Degree College of Arts & Commerce in August, 2023 2) Attended an online webinar on “ Tips on publishing Research Papers in Reputed Journals” in September,2023. Retirement: CA Jimy Wankadia retired on 31 st May, 24 after a dedicated service of more than 25 years At Jai Hind, the Accountancy Association stands as a cornerstone for students passionate about the world of finance and accounting. The objective of the association is to include an urge in students to empower themselves with the knowledge of international standards and apply the same for the benefit of the student community. Our association is dedicated to fostering a deep understanding of accounting principles and practices, while also providing a platform for networking and professional growth. Through a range of activities including workshops, seminars, and interactive events, we aim to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world application. The Accountancy Association hosted various events and seminars to enhance the personality of students and provide them with practical knowledge. 92

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 93 Content: The first seminar hosted by the association for Academic Year 2023-24 was on Personal and Financial Planning on August 1, 2023, Tuesday at 11 am. The speaker for the seminar was CA. Karan Gupta focused on topics related to loans and various types of interests. Second event was on Chat GPT and uses of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Business and Guidance on ACCA on Friday, September 15, 2023 in the AV Room. The Speaker, Professor Yogita Rawat, gave an insightful presentation on AI and its applications in the field of accountancy, advancements in chatbots and AI technologies, emphasising their impact on the accounting profession.The Accountancy Association of Jai Hind College (Autonomous) had their Accountancy Fair – ELEVATION on December 14, 2023. This was a collaboration with the E-Cell Committee. The fair was divided into five different competitions. The first competition was the IPL Auction (I like it I bought it). fusion of IPL auction drama and financial finesse where you unleash your inner tycoon, build a winning team, and master the art of fiscal strategy! There were 31 participants for the same and 6 teams. Concluding event of the association was on Union Budget which was held on February 3, 2024 ,Saturday. CA. MURTAZA Q. GHADIALI was the speaker of the event. A brief insight into various taxation schemes was given, fund allocation, tax planning etc. As we reflect on our achievements and look forward to future endeavors, the Accountancy Association at Jai Hind College remains committed to excellence in both academic and professional realms. Our vibrant community of students, faculty, and industry professionals continues to drive our objectives forward, fostering a deeper understanding of accountancy and its impact on the global economy. We are proud of the progress we've made and excited about the opportunities that lie ahead. Together, we will continue to inspire, innovate, and excel in the world of finance. Session by CA. MURTAZA Q. GHADIALI 93

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 94 Content: Head of Department: Prof. Yasmin Singaporewala Members: Prof. Evelyn Corriea, Prof. Dhriti Rathod, Prof. Afreen Mazgaonkar The Departments of BAF (Bachelor of Accounting and Finance), BBI (Bachelor of Banking and Insurance), and BFM (Bachelor of Financial Markets) at Jai Hind College have continually strived to provide students with a strong academic foundation in finance and business- related subjects while encouraging personal and professional growth outside the academic curriculum. These Departments have made significant progress over the years, ensuring students are equipped with the skills and knowledge required to excel in the competitive and evolving financial world. Their focus extends beyond academic achievements, encouraging students to participate in activities that foster both personal and professional growth. The Teacher Mentors from the English faculty were allotted to each participant who mentored the participants throughout their writing process. The International Relations Program (2023) Certificate Course, held in September 2023, offered participants an in-depth exploration of contemporary global politics and international relations. Organised and coordinated by Dr. Archana Mishra from the Department of History, the program featured renowned scholars and professionals as resource persons from institutions of repute such as ORF Mumbai, Gateway House, Association of Asia Scholars, Free Press Journal and Resource persons from Shiv Nadar University and Symbiosis, Gujarat University and from Consultants from Ministry of Defence. The course began on September 1st, 2023, with an introduction, followed by the Principal’s and HOD Ms Safina’s opening remarks. Throughout the program, various experts delved into significant themes. On September 2nd, Mr. Kashish Parpiani (RIL) introduced participants to the theory of international relations and analyzed the evolution of India-US bilateral relations. Sessions on September 4th led by Mr. RN Bhaskar explored India-China relations and the geopolitical implications of the Ukrainian crisis. The program also addressed border relations, maritime security, and regional geopolitics. On September 6th, Ms. Soumya Awasthi discussed India-China border dynamics, while Prof. Atul Bhardwaj analysed 21st-century geopolitics. Further sessions by experts like Admiral Pradeep Chauhan and Dr. Sanjay Bhardwaj expanded on maritime security and India’s neighbourhood policy. The course concluded with examinations of international law, nuclear diplomacy and feminist foreign policy. Participants were assessed through quizzes, essays, and video presentations, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of global issues and their multifaceted impacts. Kotwal Members: Ms. Sakina Garothwala, Ms. Zeenia Avari, Ms. Kajal Sankhala "Life is the canvas; Science is the brush that helps us paint it with meaning..." The Life Science Department at Jai Hind College is dedicated to fostering a deep understanding of biological sciences, equipping students with the knowledge and skills necessary for careers in research, healthcare, and environmental conservation. Through a blend of rigorous academics and hands-on experience, the department nurtures future leaders in the arena of Life Sciences. 116

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 117 Content: One of the standout moments this year was the introduction of the NEP for first-year students, which kicked off with great enthusiasm. The NEP 2020 is designed to build an education system that is deeply rooted in Indian values while also being globally relevant, preparing a generation of learners to face future challenges with confidence. The year kicked off with a vibrant Orientation for first-year students, marking an energetic and enthusiastic start for the academic term. In the Department of Life Sciences, second and third- year students warmly welcomed their juniors, setting a positive tone for the new academic journey. The Orientation aimed to equip the students with the knowledge and confidence needed to start their studies successfully and to integrate them smoothly into the academic community. The Department of Life Sciences offers various AAA courses for their students, and this year's course titled ‘Science of Sleep’ focused on understanding the concept, process and facets of sleep. It garnered strong interest, with many students from across the Biological Sciences Department enrolling and successfully completing the course. A series of institutional visits were organised to give students firsthand experience of how research is conducted at national institutes and provide opportunities to interact with research scholars and scientists. On November 30th, 2023, the TY students visited the ACTREC institute during its open day, accompanied by Ms. Kajal Sankhala. Additionally, a field visit was conducted in collaboration with the Botany Department. TY students from both departments visited Go Green nursery as part of their Applied Component curriculum. A Parent-Teacher meeting for TY students was organised on December 16th, 2023. This meeting allowed parents to gain insights into the subjects and assess their child's academic progress. The faculty continuously strives to make the curriculum engaging through innovative teaching methods. As part of their Continuous Assessment, TY students showcased a series of documentaries they filmed on important environmental topics. These documentaries were also uploaded on the YouTube channel of the department at the end of the academic year. Besides mind-maps and crosswords, the SY students presented examples of public participation through skits, poems, and raps. Additionally, FY students had the opportunity to display their artistic talents by creatively explaining various concepts using reels and models. The internships offered by the department are instrumental in shaping the aptitude and professional growth of students. Students from all bioscience departments have the opportunity to participate in summer internships, including a specialised program in the field of In Vitro Fertilization, organised in collaboration with the Embryology Academy for Research & Training (EART). Additionally, another significant internship is provided by REE Labs, focusing on the rapidly advancing field of Stem cells. These internships offer invaluable hands-on experience, equipping students with practical skills and deepening their understanding of cutting-edge scientific research. In line with our commitment to fostering a robust research culture, every faculty member is actively engaged in guiding a group of Third Year students through an intensive research project during their final semester. This hands-on experience not only enhances the students' analytical and critical thinking skills, but also prepares them for academic and professional challenges ahead. To further cultivate their research acumen, students are encouraged to showcase their work at various inter and intra-collegiate research meets, such as Avishkar 2023-24, National Science Day 117

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 118 Content: In collaboration with the Botany department, a seminar titled “Sustainable Living: Revisiting the Roots” was held on 6th February, 2024.The seminar focused on educating participants about the principles of sustainability, highlighting strategies to minimize environmental impact, conserve resources, and promote sustainable practices across different sectors. The objective was to inspire practical solutions and encourage a commitment to incorporating sustainability into everyday activities and organizational practices. The Department of Life Sciences, under the auspices of I.R.I.S, organizes an annual seminar or lecture to keep young minds updated on the latest scientific advancements. This year’s seminar, held on 10th February 2024, focused on the topic ‘Forensic Science: Behind Every Crime, There Is a Clue!’ and was attended by over 111 students from the Biosciences departments. The lectures were delivered by Ms. Riva Pocha, Ms. Sejal Gupta, Ms. Priya Dedhia, and Ms. Aloki Doshi. Ms. Pocha, Ms. Gupta, and Ms. Doshi are forensic educators at FACTS, while Ms. Dedhia is an IT security analyst and certified ethical hacker. The Department of Life Sciences takes great pride in its alumni, who are excelling in various fields. To honour their achievements, we launched a new lecture series last year titled ‘Humans of JHC – Life Sciences,’ aimed at showcasing the experiences and stories of our Life Sciences alumni. The first session took place on 28th October, 2023, featuring two of our distinguished alumni, Mr. Riddhesh Ahire and Ms. Irshikaa Sharma. Mr. Ahire discussed his MSc project on Hydra, while Ms. Sharma shared insights from her MSc research on Zebrafish. The lectures were highly engaging, offering students the valuable opportunity to connect with senior alumni who were in their shoes just a few years ago. The students' enthusiasm extended beyond these activities, culminating in the successful launch of our departmental magazine, ‘The Catalyst - Issue 4,’ under the aegis of I.R.I.S. This publication serves as a vibrant showcase of all the initiatives undertaken by the Department throughout the current academic year. It features a collection of research articles authored by our students, highlighting their scholarly achievements, alongside creative expressions such as sketches and photography. Additionally, ‘The Catalyst’ provides a platform for our faculty members to share their thoughts, making it a true reflection of the collective spirit and talents within our department. Overall, this academic year has been highly productive, allowing us to organize and execute numerous activities. The faculty and students are excited for the next academic year, aiming to maintain our momentum and achieve even more for our department's pride. Additionally, all faculty members regularly attended workshops and seminars to stay updated on recent scientific developments. The Life Sciences Department is committed to implementing similar initiatives in the future as we put “Life into Science and Science into Life”. 118

Department USP is experiential learning through initiatives such as -Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub (Under RUSA) which conducts yearlong activities, sessions, master classes etc. E- nnovate which is a 5-day long initiative consisting of speaker sessions, workshops and business competitions on a variety of relevant and upcoming topics related to the world of Business and Entrepreneurship. Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 120 Content: This year's Global Summit event partnered with renowned organizations like IDBI and Jio News, attracting a prestigious lineup of speakers, industry leaders and founders gracing the Summit with their presence which were Mr. Chandrakant Salukhe the Founder and President of SME Chamber of India, Mr. Manish Chowdhary the Co-Founder of WOW Skin Science, Mr. Vishal Gondal the Founder and Chief Executive Officer of GOQii, Mr. Argenio Antao the Chief Operating Officer at Colliers India, Mr. Sneh Vaswani the Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer of Miko, Mr. Vedant Lamba the founder of Mainstreet and Forbes 30 under 30 honouree, and Mr. Hersh Shah the CEO of IRM India Affiliate all graced the stage with their insights and expertise. MASS MEDIA Head of Department: Saiprasad Shetty and Naziya Khan Members: Mr Mohit Patel Media Lab Assistant: Prathmesh Matondkar "The camera is much more than a recording apparatus, it is a medium of influence and communication." - Daisaku Ikeda The Mass Media Department at Jai Hind College has actively engaged in a variety of events and activities during the academic year 2023-2024. These activities were aimed at enhancing students' practical exposure to media, fostering collaboration with industry professionals, and encouraging community engagement. Below is a summary of key activities undertaken by the Department. The Meeting of the Board of Studies for Mass Media was conducted on June 27th, 2023 and April 22nd, 2024, focusing on advancing the implementation of the National Education Policy. During these sessions, we discussed and adapted the program to align with the policy's guidelines, ensuring that our curriculum meets the latest educational standards and effectively addresses the evolving needs of the media industry. The Department partnered with Alliance Française de Bombay to organise a special screening event for students on August 29th, 2023. The screening featured the film ‘France’ by Bruno Dumont, offering students an immersive experience into the realm of sensational journalism, followed by an interactive discussion. The Department conducted its annual exhibit titled ‘Brand Odyssey’ on September 8th and 9th 2023, showcasing product prototypes developed by students. These prototypes represented the culmination of lessons in brand building, advertising, and marketing research, taking projects from ideation to creation. Students presented their innovative products, highlighting their understanding of market trends, and creative advertising strategies. The Annual Media Conclave titled ‘Constellation’, one of the flagship events of the department, was conducted on December 22nd and 23rd, 2023. Media professionals, including foley artists, journalists, content creators, and meme marketers, were invited to share their insights and lead panel discussions and workshops on current trends and challenges in the media industry. The conclave offered students valuable opportunities to engage with industry experts and deepen their understanding of the field. The Department organised ‘Detour’, the intercollegiate mass media festival, attracting participation from colleges across the region on January 15th and 16th, 2024. The festival included a range of competitions such as film-making, advertising campaigns, media quizzes, creative writing, photography, fashion and more, offering students a platform to showcase their talents and engage in constructive competition. Industry experts were invited to judge the events and share valuable insights. 122

By incorporating practical, hands-on learning experiences and insights from industry professionals, we ensure that our students are well-prepared for the evolving media landscape.
