Mr Hardik Mehta, Director of ‘Decoupled’ and Writer of Paatal Lok, ‘Trapped’ among other films, was an external jury member in addition to Mr Arun Varghese with experience in Direction, Editing and Screenwriting. The department in association with the Asiatic Society of Mumbai participated in a play reading event titled ‘Mumbai Traps’ curated by Ms Anju Makhija on August 18th, 2023. Through these diverse initiatives, the Mass Media Department remains committed to fostering creativity, critical thinking, and professional growth, preparing students to excel in the ever-evolving media landscape. Mr Mohit Patel had written a paper titled ‘How Artificial Intelligence is Helping the Marketing Team of Small Businesses to Scale up their Operations’ in a peer reviewed journal with an impact factor of 7.428. Manish Kharage, from the Batch of 2019, has been making a name for himself in content creation as a popular artist. He consistently produces engaging and humorous content across his platforms. Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 124 Content: MATHEMATICS Head of Department: Ms. Leena Upadhye Members: Dr Vijay Tiwari, Mr Pranil Waikar and Ms Nisha Prajapati “Mathematics is everywhere, so look around and be amazed!” National Mathematics Day is celebrated on December 22 every year to mark the birth anniversary of legendary mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan. The celebration aims at spreading the importance of Mathematics and the contributions made by Ramanujan to the subject. To commemorate this day the Department organized a quiz competition and poster presentation. A short documentary on the life and work of Ramanujan was shown. Students also staged two plays on Ramanujan. The day ended with a group discussion on “AI a boon or a bane”. Thus, the Department tried to inculcate the spirit of curiosity and exploration that mathematics needs. MICROBIOLOGY Head of Department: Ms Roonal Kataria Members: Ms Roonal Kataria, Dr Shuchita Deepak, Ms. Snehal Dabhade, Ms. Neha Ailani "To know the world, one must get intimately acquainted with its tiniest inhabitants” - Antonie Van Leeuwenhoek The Department of Microbiology was established in the year 1981 with the option of Microbiology as a major subject at the T.Y. B.Sc level. The B.Sc in Microbiology degree program at Jai Hind College allows students to gain an Introduction to the Microbiology and to explore the specific areas such as Microbial Genetics, Medical Microbiology and Immunology, Microbial Biochemistry and Bioprocess Technology. The main objective of the Department is to impart quality education in the field of Microbiology and to create skilled microbiologists to contribute to the fields of food production, pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, environment, agriculture and public health research. The Department of Microbiology aims at using Microbial research as a tool for the benefit of mankind. The Microbiology Department has conducted several activities at departmental level for the skill enhancement of the students as well as staff. The year started with scheduling BOS Meeting on June 30 , 2023 with an agenda to revise Semester I and Semester II syllabi as per NEP and Discuss the Evaluation pattern. A student activity of Water analysis of neighbouring societies conducted on August, 10, 2023 during the rainy season as water in the tanks can get contaminated. The SYBSc students performed experiments to test the microbiological quality of water. 124

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 125 Content: This activity helped the students to apply this learning concept to the benefit of the society.The societies in turn appreciated the hard work of the students and were happy to note the quality of the water sample. Another activity conducted by the Department for maintaining a healthy lifestyle is ‘Diet Planning and Management’ on September 07, 2023. The aim of the activity was to understand the importance of healthy eating habits, and a balanced diet and to learn the concept of constructing meal plans based on the needs of an individual. This was an interactive session planned for students to help students build their own diet charts keeping in mind all the necessities of a balanced diet. Students enjoyed the session where they were able to calculate their caloric intake and plan their own meal based on their daily requirements. To acquaint the students with the practical knowledge about the wastewater treatment processes, One-day Field Visit to the MCGM water treatment plant was scheduled on September 13, 2023. The activity proved very beneficial to students as they gained a deeper understanding of the concepts related to waste water treatment. They appreciated the opportunity to witness the practical application of water treatment technologies firsthand, which enhanced their comprehension significantly. To enhance industrial skills and boost the students' experience, the Department conducted hands-on training sessions titled as ‘Skill Development In Food And Pharma Technology’ from October 28 to November 3, 2023. Students learned designing of growth media, executing aseptic techniques and optimising bioreactor conditions. They were also able to examine the fermentation parameters and the quality of the product formed. Students also evaluated and conducted antimicrobial assays and analysed the results using statistical analysis.This year, the students were taken to visit The Advanced Centre for Treatment,Research & Education (ACTREC) on November 30, 2023 to inculcate the inquisitiveness among the students for research and Higher education. Students intricately understood about the in vitro studies carried out in model animals. The experiments that were carried out were observed by the students. Apart from lab knowledge, the students even learnt about the different opportunities and fellowships from the PhD students working in the institution The Department is actively involved in taking up consultancy services for the MSc and PhD students for completion of their dissertation work. The consultancy offers the institute the opportunity to conduct and expand research in interdisciplinary areas which can lead to new collaborations, grants and publications. The Department actively participated in Science Open Day and Xplore, 2024 organised by the College. A guided tour of the Microbiology Laboratory provided a behind-the- scenes glimpse into the state-of-the-art facilities available at Jai Hind College, fostering interest and curiosity among attendees. This exhibition successfully engaged students in meaningful discussions, encouraging them to consider pursuing careers in microbiology and related fields. Department focuses not just on learning but celebrates learning by organising the Department Fest. The annual fest FESTCHERICHIA’ 24 - MICRO APOCALYPSE was conducted on January 06, 2023, aimed to illuminate lesser-known biological science streams, often overshadowed by mainstream career courses. It served as an interdisciplinary platform, drawing participants from colleges across Mumbai. The Departmental Newsletter - MICRO-BULLETIN was also released in this event. The Department is actively involved in spreading science awareness for which the Science Awareness Program was scheduled on February 8, 2024. It inspires the young minds to take pure science subjects as a career option. It opened the minds of the school students to various fields in Microbiology. As the learning is a continuous process, specialised visits are conducted by the department. A Visit to Breach Candy Hospital Clinical Microbiology Laboratory on March 16, 2024. Students gained a deeper understanding of microbiology testing methodologies, including specimen processing, culture techniques, and culture identification methods.They could understand the general workflow of a clinical microbiology laboratory and a better understanding of the automated equipment and procedures that are commonly employed. 125

Besides various activities conducted, students participated in inter-collegiate fests and came back with laurels and even on the research front, our students have excelled. The year began with a guest lecture on ‘Ethics of the Beauty Industry’ delivered by our alumnus Ms. Nidhi Lulla jointly organized by Women’s Development Cell (WDC) on August 4, 2023 which was very well received by students. Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 134 Content: A five-part Workshop Series on ‘Understanding Contemporary Politics’ was held from August 2, 2023 to August 30, 2023, in collaboration with One Future Collective (OFC), a non-profit working for fostering youth leadership and feminist causes. The sessions covered a comprehensive array of topics which included Constitutional and Human Rights, an Introduction to Public Policy, gender justice, climate change, and careers in Public Policy. The attendees were thus provided the basic theoretical tools to actively engage in critical debates in politics and policy. Students from the Department of Political Science attended a talk on G20: The India Way delivered by Dr. Samir Saran at Sophia College for Women (Autonomous) on 29th August, 2023. The talk was organised collaboratively by the Departments of Political Science at Jai Hind College, St. Xavier’s College (Autonomous) and the International Relations Club of Sophia College for Women. The speaker put forth the perspective of the Global South in calling for a need to meaningfully restructure the multilateral system, to better reflect the current priorities. The role that India’s presidency of the G20 could play in facilitating this transition was the keynote of the talk. The Annual Students’ Research Seminar was held on January 11, 2024. The theme for this year’s seminar was ‘Digital Politics: Power, Governance and Participation in the Digital Age’. Participants explored different areas of application of digital technologies in Public Policy as well the challenges arising from the digitisation of political processes. Issues of digital diplomacy, political polarisation on digital media, digital queer activism, and the use of digital tools for reforming the legal system were some of the topics examined by the paper-presenters. Jyothi Members: Dr. Ruchi Dubey Chaturvedi, Dr. Freyana Shinde The Psychology Department has had a dynamic and successful year in implementing activities in our Department. It was marked by a variety of activities that engaged both the mind and the heart. From insightful guest lectures and interactive workshops to hands-on research projects and community outreach. The Certificate Course for Life Skills (CCLS) conducted from July to August 2023 is an insightful and intellectually stimulating course conducted by the psychology department of Jai Hind College. The objective was to enlighten students beyond their usual curriculum and give an enlightening understanding on basic life skills such as decision making, coping mechanism and management skills. Our Annual Psychology festival ‘Psyc Insight’ which was held on September 3 and 4, 2023, aimed to create an environment of fun filled learning with games such as creating a business idea, treasure hunt based on psychological clues and to enhance psychological knowledge. Appreciating different schools of thought of Psychology, different perspectives, Psychologist’s viewpoints, psychology based therapies and different countries perspectives regarding mental health. The seminar was conducted on the occasion of 'World Suicide Prevention Day' titled ‘Suicide prevention - Creating hope through action’ which began with the introduction of the esteemed resource person, Dr. Avinash Desouza. The speaker maintained that every suicide is preventable and that being equipped with the necessary information, people can assist individuals with suicidal ideation to receive vital help from mental health professionals. The speaker also stated that individuals who receive training via the Gatekeeping Program are instructed in a scientific manner which equips them to be of assistance to individuals with suicidal tendencies by introduction to a mental health practitioner, thus, saving a life. Individuals with suicidal tendencies require hope and reassurance. These trained individuals are expected to make them realise that suicide is not a solution to the problem and there is hope to solve the problem they are faced with. The speaker also stressed upon the fact that post identification of individual with suicidal ideation (SI), it is important to be an active listener as these individuals require a patient, non-judgemental ear. He recommended that these individuals should be accompanied most of the time. A “no suicide pact” was another recommendation made by the speaker, where individuals with SI are to contact the trained person when feeling suicidal, and under no circumstances make a suicidal attempt. The next was the Certificate Course for Research Methodology (CCRM), held in the months of January and February 2024.The objective was to enlighten students beyond their usual curriculum and give them a brief but profound glimpse into the usage of softwares for research methodology. The curriculum for each of the levels included basics of research and report writing, qualitative analysis tools and quantitative analysis tools with respect to JASP and Excel. A research Convention for our TYBA psychology major students, ‘Manthan’ was held on February 8,9, and 10, 2024. The primary objectives included exploring cutting-edge research methodologies, novel psychological phenomena, ground-breaking findings, replicating and testing classical concepts and experiments of psychology and fostering a deeper understanding of the complex interplay of various psychological variables to push the boundaries of human understanding, 136

The workshop began with a quick round of 'Would You Rather' to break the ice with the participants and to make them feel comfortable. The workshop then progressed wherein the volunteers went on to discuss what Stress is and different types of Stress with the aim of making it relatable and very easy to comprehend. The workshop then adeptly transitioned into the end with complete focus on participants with activities like Charades and 4- 7- 8 breathing exercises with an aim to help the participants tune in with their stressors. Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 138 Content: ‘Basics of Excel’ was a webinar delivered by alumni Mr. Soochit Bhatt. The session aimed to familiarize students with Microsoft Excel, emphasizing its utility in statistical analysis and research data management. The workshop was attended by 41 students, all of whom are budding psychology professionals keen on enhancing their technical skills. The workshop commenced with an overview of Excel’s interface, followed by a demonstration of basic functions such as data entry, sorting, and filtering. Students were introduced to essential statistical functions, including mean, median, standard deviation, and how to use Excel for creating descriptive statistics. The facilitator also covered the application of Excel in data visualization, teaching students how to generate histograms, pie charts, and scatter plots, which are crucial for presenting research findings effectively. Practical exercises were integrated into the workshop, allowing students to apply what they had learned by working with sample datasets. This hands-on experience was invaluable in reinforcing their understanding of Excel’s capabilities in handling research data. The workshop concluded with a discussion on the importance of Excel in conducting psychological research, data interpretation, and report writing. Guest speakers included Ms. Mona Cheriyan, who stressed integrating green investment topics into curricula; Mr. Amit Madhan, who discussed ESG frameworks; Mr.Mani Mahesh Aurora, who shared insights on sustainable rural tourism; Mr. Rahul Himalayan, who presented IRCTC's green initiatives; and Mr. Sandeep Kotak, who talked about MURA MICE's green efforts and the need for skills in this area. Voyage Fest 2023, organized by the BVoc Travel and Tourism Management Department, is an annual celebration held around World Tourism Day. Launched three years ago out of a passion for travel, Voyage Fest features a variety of events such as dance, drama, fashion shows, singing, student seminars, and symposiums. This year, student heads Sanika Talkar, Shubh Zaveri, Mubeen Khan, and Ayaan Ahmed led the festival, introducing a new addition: the Tourism Fair 'Paryatan Haat.' Held on September 26-27, 2023, Paryatan Haat included an exhibition with travel companies, art, food stalls, and live demos, inaugurated by Principal Dr. Vijay Dabholkar. The event provided students with a platform to showcase their organizational and leadership skills. Students completed a Wine Tourism Course from 02/02/2024 – 15/02/2024, covering global wine regions, vineyard management, and marketing strategies. The course included a visit to Sula Vineyards in Nashik, where students toured the vineyards, observed winemaking, and participated in a wine-tasting session. This experience provided practical insights into the wine tourism industry and its opportunities. The Convocation celebrated the graduating students' achievements with a well-organized program, including an invocation, degree awards, and inspiring speeches. Organized by FY and SY BVoc students, the event marked a significant milestone and set an optimistic tone for the graduates' futures. The FY BVoc TTM embarked on an educational trip to Elephanta Caves, exploring the ancient rock-cut temples and gaining insights into their historical and cultural significance. Additionally, all BVoc TTM students enjoyed an adventure trip to Khopoli, which included engaging outdoor activities and team- building experiences. The heritage walk to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya provided a deep dive into Mumbai's rich history and art. These trips offered students valuable learning experiences outside the classroom, blending education with exploration and adventure. The students of TYBVOC TTM had dissertation for International and National Travel Circuits. Dr Archana Mishra along with visiting faculty Ms. Shardha Prabhu provided mentoring to the students. Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 146 Content: Faculty Advisor : Dr. Rakhi Sharma Student Secretary : Rupanki Dedhia Student Joint Secretary : Aashna Doctor, Hetvi Sheth Enactus is a global, student-driven organization that uses entrepreneurial action to transform lives and shape a better, more sustainable world. Through collaborative projects, Enactus empowers students to develop innovative solutions that address pressing social issues, combining business skills with a passion for change. At Jai Hind College, Enactus has been an integral part of our journey and has been a member of Enactus since 2015, providing a platform to apply our knowledge and skills for social impact. The initiative helps students engage with real-world challenges and fosters leadership, teamwork, and a spirit of community service. The projects undertaken have tackled a wide range of issues including education, waste management and women’s empowerment. It's a unique opportunity that has enriched our academic and personal growth. TECH SHIKSHA An initiative which was started with the objective of providing basic education to the underprivileged children through the online medium during COVID has been continued and is now a part of the offline mode of teaching focusing primarily on the need of bridging the gap between their reality and a future filled with opportunity. In the year 2023-24, Tech Shiksha strived to create that world for the underprivileged children of Mumbai, especially those in Dharavi. By recognizing the number of children whose needs are uncatered to, concentrating in the slum areas with a population of 300,000 it provides an opportunity and interacts with 100 of these children with an aim to improve their lifestyle and providing education and other activities for their overall development. As a part of this project we collaborated with the Community Outreach Programme (CORP) and taught the children at the Gulalwadi Hindi BMC School. We successfully targeted around 100 students with the age ranging from 5-15 with 30+ volunteers who helped the children in every way and taught them fundamentals of english, math and also engaged in extra curriculars like arts, craft and dance. The visits were scheduled every Saturday for a span of two months during the course of which we developed a strong connection with them. With its motive of Teach while you learn, Learn while you teach, it focuses on education through volunteerism. The volunteers have used different and engaging ways of teaching to make the process and journey of imparting education enjoyable and memorable. They have ensured access to quality education despite the lack of quality resources and have achieved several goals. The main goals achieved was that of holistic development which helps in nurturing a well-rounded skillset that goes beyond academics, fostering creativity and emotional intelligence and confidence building which was attained through interactive sessions and positive reinforcement to empower children to believe in themselves and their abilities ENACTUS, JAI HIND COLLEGE 146

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 147 Content: and become individuals who can stand up for themselves in the world. Another benefit that was accomplished was the focus on critical thinking which was developed through the sessions we had with the children wherein they had to take part in interpersonal projects and problem solving exercises designed to make them think outside the box and come up with creative solutions. The whole project was centered around the sustainable development goals of No Poverty, Quality Education and Reduced Inequalities. In conclusion Tech Shiksha helps overcome the difficulties faced by millions of children whose eye sparkle with curiosity and want to learn and grow by connecting them with volunteers and NGOs serving this purpose, and is a small step towards a more literate world. THE ENTREPRENEURSHIP CELL AND SKILL HUB Chairperson: Dr Rakhi Sharma Student Secretary: Brinda Dhorda The Institution’s Innovation Council (IIC) was launched by the Ministry of Education of the Government of India in collaboration with AICTE. The purpose of it is to systematically foster the culture of innovation and the start-up ecosystem in all Higher Education Institutions across the country. Jai Hind College's E-Cell and Skill Hub has joined hands with IIC to foster the growing entrepreneurial ecosystem. The E-Cell and Skill Hub operates with an arrant focus of transforming job-seekers into the job-makers of tomorrow through consistent re-skilling and empirical learning opportunities. It conducts speaker sessions, workshops and business competitions throughout the year ensuring continuous learning, and organizes its flagship event, The Global Entrepreneurship Summit. This year it conducted 13 insightful sessions and events on diverse topics like Rise of Cloud Kitchens in India, Rural Marketing Strategies and Implementation, The Rise of Indian Startups: Drivers, Challenges and Opportunities, Creative and Content Marketing and many more, benefitting more than 2000 students and helping them upskill. These sessions were conducted by industry experts like Mr. Nishant Jhaveri the Co-Founder at Nino Foods, Mr. Nirvaan Birla the Managing Director at Birla Brainiacs and Birla Open Minds, Mr. Pradeep Malu the Mentor of Change at NITI Aayog and an accomplished Startup & Business Mentor, Ms. Tara Kapur the India Marketing Head, Duolingo English Test &atop Content Development voice and many more. The Prototype Demonstration, an event organized by the Cell helped attendees put their business idea into a practical and physical model, by making a prototype for their product/service so that students could understand how people are reacting to their business idea 147

Faculty Mentor: Dr Rakhi Sharma Student Secretary: Aahansh Bole Deputy Secretaries: Karan Patil and Kuldeep Singh Rajpurohit The Jai Hind College Incubator and Accelerator Centre (IAC) serves as a dynamic hub of innovation, dedicated to fostering and empowering entrepreneurial development among students. Through a combination of strategic mentorship, networking opportunities, tailored workshops, and a robust support system, the IAC accelerates the growth of student-led startups, enabling them to scale their ventures effectively and create a meaningful impact. Below is a comprehensive overview of the IAC’s initiatives, milestones, and areas of focus during the 2023-2024 academic year, showcasing its commitment to nurturing young innovators. Vision and Mission of the IAC: The IAC at Jai Hind College is committed to developing a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship by creating a supportive ecosystem for student startups. The primary objectives of the IAC include: Empowering Students: The IAC aims to empower young entrepreneurs to take their ideas from the conceptual stage to the market through structured mentorship, access to resources, and exposure to industry leaders. Fostering Collaboration: The center is committed to building a strong network of student entrepreneurs and fostering collaboration among startups to create synergies and drive innovation. Accelerating Growth: Through tailored initiatives, workshops, and mentoring programs, the IAC focuses on accelerating the growth trajectory of startups, ensuring that student- led ventures can effectively scale and thrive in competitive markets. Major Milestones and Events of 2023-2024 1. Cohort Induction Ceremony (July 2023): The IAC welcomed its 5th cohort of startups with an induction ceremony designed to inspire and orient the incoming batch. This event marked the beginning of their entrepreneurial journey, providing a glimpse into the programs and support structures available to help them succeed. 2. Visit from Terna College (August 2023): The IAC hosted a delegation of students from Terna Engineering College, Navi Mumbai. This interactive session allowed the visiting students to engage with Jai Hind College’s incubated startups, gaining valuable insights into the practical workings of the incubation process, the challenges faced by entrepreneurs, and the opportunities available for growth and innovation. 3. Intellectus: Innovation Hackathon (September 2023): Continuing its tradition of fostering creativity and problem-solving, the IAC organized the Intellectus Innovation Hackathon. This event brought together aspiring entrepreneurs and innovators to solve complex real- world problems through technological and creative solutions. Student Secretary: Aahansh Bole Co-ordinator: Dr Sreela Dasgupta Co-coordinator: Ms. Niloufer Kotwal Deputy Coordinator: Dr Reema Julka The primary focus of IQAC in the academic session 2023-24 was geared towards documentation and compilation of data for the 4th cycle of NAAC Accreditation process. The task was mammoth in proportion as the data encompassed the previous 5 years from 2018-219 to 2022-23. To facilitate the process, a separate NAAC SSR team was formed wherein each quantitative query was assigned to a specific faculty. This ensured a targeted approach with faster results and greater accuracy. The team members were assigned a deadline to complete the task. In addition, a core committee was created with a dedicated responsibility to verify the documents collected. As the entire submission process was online, a technical team was also formed to upload the data and create the requisite links for additional data. All this was closely coordinated with the Website Committee as a lot of the data required to be uploaded on the College portal. The NEP was successfully implemented in accordance with the curricular framework of the college for the first year of undergraduate and postgraduate programs. The Board of Studies meetings were subsequently held for the implementation of NEP in the 2nd year of all programs for the academic session 2024-25. Feedback was also collected from all students as well as faculty to understand lacunae, if any, in the 1st year of implementation. MARATHI VANGMAY MANDAL NATIONAL SERVICE SCHEME UNIT (NSS) UNIT Coordinator: Dr Balakrishna Rongali General Secretary: Aditi Shedge Joint Secretary: Manthan Bramhankar Let us introduce to you, the National Service Scheme (NSS) Unit of Jai Hind College, a platform for students to actively engage with their communities and contribute to nation- building. Our NSS unit is driven by the belief that young people have the power to bring about positive change, highlighted by our motto, ‘Not Me, But You’. Through various social service initiatives, we strive to instill a sense of responsibility and civic duty in our volunteers. Our mission extends beyond the college campus, aiming to deepen students' awareness of various social issues and guide them in becoming active, responsible members of the broader community. 158

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 159 Content: 159 The NSS Unit has emphasized on Education throughout its tenure, which illustrates our shared efforts in bringing about radical changes as well as spreading awareness about the current education system of the country. It has become one of the most dynamic units in the college, known for its enthusiastic participation in a wide range of activities aimed at social upliftment, educational development, and environmental conservation, among other things. With the support of a dedicated team of one hundred volunteers, our General Secretary Aditi and Joint Secretary Manthan, we are committed towards creating a lasting impression on the social activities of our college. In January 2024, volunteers from the National Service Scheme (NSS) Unit of Jai Hind College embarked on a week-long residential camp in the serene area of Usgaon, christened with the theme “Education”. This camp is more than just an excursion; it’s an intensive community service project where volunteers work together to make a meaningful impact on the local area. The NSS Unit made notable contributions to Usgaon, including cleaning the Usgaon dam, interacting with students of the local school, and improving sanitation by cleaning cow sheds. The commitment to holistic community development was further demonstrated through interactive sessions during the camp. To keep the energy high, the volunteers organized a range of competitions, showcasing talents in sports, singing, street plays, dance, and delivering thought-provoking speeches. The friendly yet competitive atmosphere fostered respect and cooperation among participants, adding to the camp’s vibrancy. The annual residential camp in Usgaon became a hub of positive change, merging community service with dynamic interactions and spirited competitions. It beautifully encapsulated the NSS Unit’s dedication to community welfare and comprehensive development. The NSS Day at Jai Hind College was celebrated with great passion, representing the values and achievements of the NSS unit. This annual event is a testament to the dedication and hard work of the students involved in various community service activities throughout the year. The celebration not only honors the spirit of selfless service but also reflects on the impact of the NSS unit’s initiatives and inspires continued commitment to social welfare. The celebration was marked by a series of cultural performances by students, showcasing their diverse talents, including music and dance performances, as well as a thought- provoking street play. Through cultural performance and various interactive activities, NSS Day highlighted the impact of the NSS unit’s initiatives and reinforced the importance of selfless service in building a better society. As the event concluded, there was a renewed sense of commitment among the volunteers, who left with a greater appreciation for their role in making a difference in their communities. On the occasion of India's 76th Independence Day, the NSS unit of Jai Hind College organized a series of events that celebrated patriotism and social responsibility with great enthusiasm. The day began with the traditional flag hoisting, attended by approximately 60 children, setting an uplifting tone for the day’s activities. A highlight was the short poem by a college alumnus, which evoked feelings of patriotism and love for the nation in everyone’s hearts. Following the reflections on independence, the event featured an energetic dance performance by the NSS unit, culminating in an inspiring speech by the Honorable Principal Dr Vijay Dabholkar. A visit to our adopted area in Dharavi included a thought- provoking street play that addressed societal prejudices. The day concluded with a communal celebration that united diverse voices in a harmonious expression of freedom and community spirit.

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 160 Content: 160 In addition, the volunteers organized a spirited Republic Day celebration, reflecting democratic values and community engagement. The event began with a flag-hoisting ceremony led by the Honorable Principal. The NSS unit presented an engaging street play which was highly relatable to the modern generation. The cultural segment was filled with patriotic performances, including dance and singing, with eloquent contributions from Student Leaders Ms. Aditi and Mr. Manthan. After the campus festivities, volunteers visited Dharavi to conduct impactful door-to-door awareness campaigns on menstruation hygiene. The day ended with heartfelt farewells and expressions of gratitude, underscoring the NSS commitment to driving change and raising awareness beyond the College. On November 30, 2023, the National Service Scheme Unit of Jai Hind College hosted its Annual Blood Donation Drive, transforming the campus into a center of compassion and community engagement. The event, inaugurated by Dr. Vijay Dabholkar, the College Principal, aimed not only at collecting blood but also at creating a positive impact on the college and society. Volunteers, equipped with creative slogans, spread awareness at Churchgate Station, Marine Drive, and other locations, significantly increasing participation. The enthusiasm and dedication of the volunteers were palpable throughout the day, leading to 102 successful donations and setting a commendable example. This success highlighted the importance of such initiatives and demonstrated the power of collective efforts in promoting blood donation and saving lives. The vibrant atmosphere, combined with the heartfelt commitment of volunteers and donors, marked the blood donation drive as a major achievement for Jai Hind College's NSS unit. In this exceptional year at Jai Hind College, the NSS Unit has been a symbol of civic engagement and social responsibility. From the dynamic celebration of NSS Day, emphasizing the significance of the NSS unit, to the impactful week-long residential camp in Usgaon that combined service with lively competitions, the NSS Unit has embodied comprehensive community development. The Independence and Republic Day celebrations reflected a blend of patriotism and social responsibility, leaving a lasting impression. The year’s highlight was the outstanding success of the Annual Blood Donation Drive, where 102 donors, driven by enthusiasm, showcased the power of collective compassion. This year highlights the NSS Unit's unwavering dedication to developing responsible and empathetic leaders for a better and brighter tomorrow. We are delighted to announce that 23 NSS volunteers of Academic Years 22-23 and 23-24 have been awarded certificates by the Ministry of Youth Affairs, Government of India. This remarkable achievement would not have been possible without the dedicated efforts of our NSS Core Committee and the unwavering commitment of our volunteers and Teachers. The NSS team also won First Place at the University of Mumbai NSS Street Play Competition, at the University level. We are proud of our students.

The organization also celebrates international days and hosts entertainment activities to keep members involved. The theme for the year was “The Spark Within” which means RCJC is that platform which gives its members the opportunities to ignite the spark they have within them . Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 162 Content: Pinnacle: Pinnacle 2023 was the 29th Installation Ceremony of the Rotaract Club of Jai Hind College held on August 18, 2023. It marked the departure of Rtr. Khushi Shetty - President 2022-23 and her team, and the incoming of Rtr. Vedika Bansal President 2023-24 and her team. The grand affair included farewells of 5 past members and the induction of 5 Core Team members and 23 Directors for the year 2023-24. The event was graced with the presence of Rtn. Siddhant and Rtn. Nikhil as Guests of Honour and Adv. Phf. Rtn. Rtr. DRR Nikhita Sunil as the Chief Guest. It recorded an attendance of 230 members. Zodiak Fest 2023 was conducted on September 11, 2023. It was a day-long cultural event that focused on honing the different talents of the young Rotaractors. The theme for this fest was ‘The Gala Affair’, celebrating all the big festivals and fiestas of the world. The fest saw participation of four distinct Rotaract Clubs from Rotaract District 3141. The events took place in the basement of Jai Hind College, and consisted of the Dance Show, the Fashion Show, and a talent round known as Mr. & Ms. Zodiak. Along with the events, there was also a flea market set up in the Foyer of the college, that had stalls setHealth up by small businesses and established ventures alike. There was a plethora of small-scale businesses that were selling items like jewelry, tote bags, baked goodies, ice popsicles and much more. A lot of them were businesses started by the alumni of Jai Hind College. The flea market provided a platform for these businesses to grow and establish themselves in a better manner. All the businesses had an amazing experience, with a few getting sold out as well. The participating Rotaract Clubs had a blast performing in all events. The winner of the fest was the Rotaract Club of M. L. Dahanukar College, while the Rotaract Club of Churchgate bagged the runners-up position. Silent Saturdays: It was a month-long community service initiative conducted during the month of november. The initiative focused on four main causes: Health, Hunger, Education and Safety. The team had adopted a slum in Bandra and carried out daily donation drives of basic necessities. Every Saturday the team conducted a street play based on the four causes. At the end of the month, the team performed one act that made a huge impact, to denote the highlight of Silent Saturdays 2023. They donated a sewing machine and basic necessities to a widow with two young daughters, who had recently lost her husband, along with all the savings in hospital bills. The items provided to her helped her start afresh and get back on her feet to support her daughters and herself. The Project impacted 195+ families, 1500+ people via street play and overall a total of 2100+ lives through the project. Word War I was a project we conducted for school students. It was an event conducted on February 10th, 2024, where we invited Schools from around Mumbai to participate in a series of events that focused on Literary arts, Fine arts, and the General Knowledge of the students. The competition had events like Ex Tempore, General Knowledge Quiz, Clay Modelling and more. The event was conducted in the basement of Jai Hind College, and the premises were creatively decorated according to the theme of Harry Potter. There was a panel of esteemed judges invited to judge each event, who also provided valuable knowledge and insights to the students post each event. The competition was won by Byramjee Jeejeebhoy Parsee Charitable School, while the runners-up was bagged by the Childrens’ Academy, Thakur Village. Cheekh 2024 was a series of initiatives undertaken during International Womens’ Week, in an attempt to raise a voice against Women’s Trafficking. This year, the event consisted of a flashmob that depicted three different instances of how young girls end up in the racket of women trafficking, and how they together fought against it and punished the perpetrators of the heinous crime. These flash mobs were performed at public spaces like malls and 162

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 163 Content: railway stations to reach the maximum audience. The team also shot a short film that highlighted 3 stories, each highlighting a traumatic event that affected the mental health of the woman in severe ways, which led to each woman eventually taking their life. Additionally, the team also did a photo shoot, and posted the shots on social media pages. There was an open mic also conducted, which gave people a chance to express their views on the cause. By the end of the year we had conducted over 150+ projects, had won 62 certificates and 9 trophies at the Academy for Awarding Rotaract Achievements night in the entire Rotaract District 3141, and had over 150+ members in the Rotaract movement. The team indeed had one of the best years, improving in all aspects. The Team of 2024-25 aspires to continue the legacy and improve, scale, and achieve higher summits. The RCJC aims at creating more and more impact as the years pass by, making it a safe space for anyone and everyone. The Training and Placement Cell of Jai Hind College is a pivotal resource for students, dedicated to bridging the gap between academic learning and industry expectations. Founded with the collective vision and support of esteemed faculty, the cell aims to assist students in securing internships and placement opportunities, as well as offering comprehensive training programs that prepare them for the challenges of the corporate world. Jai Hind College graduates are well-prepared and skilled, bringing a variety of competencies to their future employers, which makes them highly valued in the professional sphere. The 2023-24 academic year was a landmark period for the Training and Placement Cell, as it successfully brought companies to campus for placement recruitment, securing roles for the graduating batch across diverse domains. These companies included prestigious names such as Ernst & Young, KPMG, ICICI Bank, Deutsche Bank, Schbang, and Zomato, among others, and they offered opportunities in departments spanning banking, portfolio management, social media and marketing, and human resource management. The Training and Placement Cell operates under a structure that ensures each department’s unique needs are met effectively. Faculty coordinators and a well-organized student leadership team work in tandem to handle placements. This team structure facilitates comprehensive support for students across all courses, ensuring seamless organization, coordination, and execution of activities. 163

Roles and Responsibilities: Facilitation of Key Processes: The Student Council facilitates and ensures the conduct of a smooth admission process, involving answering general queries of parents/guardians and prospective students, collection of relevant documents and any other guidance requested from time to time. The objectives of the Women’s Development Cell include gender sensitization; healthcare; empowerment; the education of the girls through community interface; social awareness, and exploring entrepreneurship opportunities. Jai Hind has a very strong participation in events of the WDC, and our students are passionate about causes related to women’s development and gender sensitization. This year, we took up the theme of ‘Beauty Within’ and considered the Beauty Industry and its influence on society at large. Our Inaugural event was a guest lecture on “Ethics of the Beauty Industry” delivered by our alumna Nidhi Lulla on August 4, 2023. The objective of the guest lecture was to critically examine the ethical challenges within the beauty industry, focusing on issues such as consumerism, body image, and sustainability, while encouraging students to reflect on their roles as both consumers and future professionals in this field. Ms. Lulla, a seasoned expert in the beauty and fashion industry, provided the students with an in-depth analysis of the ethical dilemmas that are prevalent in the beauty sector today. The session began with an overview of the beauty industry’s evolution, highlighting how cultural and societal standards of beauty have shifted over time. Ms. Lulla then delved into the ethical concerns associated with these changes, such as the impact of advertising on body image, the exploitation of workers in production chains, and the environmental consequences of beauty products. Ms. Lulla also addressed the concept of "ethical consumerism," emphasising the importance of making informed choices about the products we use and the brands we support. She shared insights on the growing trend of sustainable and cruelty-free beauty, encouraging students to think critically about their purchasing decisions and the broader impact of the beauty industry on society and the environment. The lecture was interactive, with students posing questions and engaging in discussions about their own experiences and observations. Ms. Lulla's expertise and passion for ethical practices in the beauty industry were evident, making the session both informative and thought-provoking. wellness. DISCUSSION CLUBS Faculty in Charge: Dr Deepna Rao Students: Aastha Singh (Head), Gopal Nambiar (Sub-head), Pujita Arora (Sub-head), Aarushi Morakhia (Sub-head), Revati Salunkhe, Shaina Bedi and Ria Punjabi (Co- ordinators) The Novel Room is an affiliated Discussion Club of the English Association, and its ebullient spaces invite and engage just about anyone and everyone with a curious mind and a talkative temperament. The team in charge of curating the Novel Room sessions has one intention: to be as interactive and interlocutory as possible and give the audiences the literary experiences of a lifetime. Its core activities involve the discovery, exploration, interpretation and analysis of the literary genre as a whole. The Novel Room is dedicated to curating and conducting student-friendly sessions that aid in holistic development. The Government of India, Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) introduced the New Education Policy (NEP) in 2020, calling for imaginative and flexible co-curricular activities to be included into Degree Courses for students. Co-curricular Courses (CC) can be obtained via multiple activities such as Health and Wellness, Yoga Education, Sports, Fine/Applied/Performing Arts, Cultural Activities, etc. at the convenience, preference, and THE NOVEL ROOM (UNDER THE AEGIS OF THE DEPARTMENT OF ENGLISH) 172

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 173 Content: necessity of the students. The Novel Room conducts sessions that fall under the Cultural and Extra-curricular level. Learning (Expected) Objectives of Novel Room: To foster learning beyond traditional literature and inculcate critical thinking in students beyond classroom learning. To encourage public speaking and develop communication skills among students. To familiarise students of varying courses with the broad scope of literature and its relevance in society and daily life. To encourage healthy and regular reading among students. To promote research and analysis through deep cultural, political, psychological, historical and social discussions relevant in the context of literature. To develop vocabulary, reading, writing, listening and speaking skills of students through various tasks and activities assigned to them in every session. To facilitate imaginative and flexible interactions in regard to literature that align with the New Education Policy (NEP). Duration and Frequency: The Novel Room conducts 10 sessions throughout the year. These include bi-monthly sessions, each being around 2-3 hours. The Novel Room organised 10 events in the Academic Year 2023-24, ranging between 2-3 hours per session. Each session also included an additional assignment that had to be submitted by each participant. Event I / Inaugatory Session – “Animated Characters: From Monochrome to Technicolor” The Inaugatory Event of the Novel Room was in collaboration with the Film Room, inculcating one of the most popular, student-friendly topics– comic and graphic novels in literature. Students were exposed to various discussion topics such as the Historical Evolution of Comics, Popularization of Queer Identity, the narrative of good v/s evil, the contribution of Indian Comics to Literature, and the mention of popular graphic novels of Barbie and Dragon Ball Z. The session was also marked by Film Room screenings of multiple nostalgic media. Event II: “To the Reader, For the Readers: Storytelling through Epistolaries” The second session of the novel room revolved around the concept of epistolaries and dove deep into the evolution, adaptations and significance of epistolaries in literature. From the history to modern digital use of letters with emphasis on romance and poetry, the participants were engaged in various in-depth discussions about the same. Further, the limitations and shortcomings of epistolaries were discussed and the students were allotted an assignment at the end of the session. Event III: “Mythologies: From Gods to Gargoyles” The third event of the Novel Room was one of the few online events held during the academic year, focusing on mythology as an important aspect of literature. In order to keep the audience engaged, the event was marked with games to keep the minds of the audience fresh. From bringing up the beloved Percy Jackson series to the underrated nature of Indian Mythology and the addition of the discovery of Christian, Norse and Mesopotamian Mythology, the third session of the Novel Room was one of the most well- 173

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 174 Content: received online session, concluding with thoughtful discussion on the influence of mythology in modern-day life. Event IV: “Symphony of the Pages: Music & Literature” The fourth event of the Novel Room was held offline and centred around the theme of music and literature. It was an interactive session where students talked about their favourite artists, analysed the meanings of various lyrics and compared the lyrics used in different languages, and contexts. There was a focus on translations and meanings of lyrics with reference to culture. Students also engaged in a fun translation activity where they had to try to directly translate the given lyrics from one language to another. Event V: “Twice Upon a Time: Fairy Tales Retold” The fifth event of the Novel Room aimed to highlight fairytales from a literary perspective. The audience discussed the common fairy tales they grew up with, to their different adaptations that had arisen as society progressed. From Ethnic Fairytales to the true origins behind the fantastical story of Snow White and the Moral Ambiguity within fairytales, the Novel Room’s fifth session was filled with evoking long-buried childhood memories. Event VI: “Reading Between the Miles: A Literary Expedition” Conducted online, it took travel as a part of literature, discussed in detail along with a variety of topics, such as evolution of one’s personality through travel, fantasy travel literature used in books such as TinTin and Geronimo Stilton, travel and culture including immaterial culture such as places created in books that one is aware of but does not exist. The audience resonated with these experiences and could relate with the travel imagery created in these books. Event VII: “The Novel Room x Kalki: The Binary Breaker” The seventh session of the Novel Room welcomed Abhishek Lamba, one of the long- standing alumnus of Jai Hind College during “Kalki: An Eternal Expression.” Deviating from the regular nature of the Novel Room sessions, event seven saw a receptive audience enjoy interactive games challenging ‘Mr. Patriarchy’ or the traditional notion of gender being thoroughly assigned since birth. Thanks to Abhishek Lamba, the audience were able to engage in games and conversations that made us all put on our creative-thinking caps while encompassing the spirit of being true to oneself. Event VIII: T(EA) Series - Event I The eighth session of the Novel Room and the first session of the T(EA) Series (wordplay on the company T-Series implying a pun the spilling of “tea” with the EA i.e English Association). Held online, it was one of the most interactive sessions where students could freely express their opinions and debate on controversial topics. Some of these topics were about “cancel culture”: whether artists should be cancelled for their unpopular opinions and if that would affect their fanbases; queer interpretations of historical texts, the possibility of misinterpretations of queer characters in the time when it was not as accepted. Event IX: T(EA) Series - Event II Event Nine of the Novel Room was a successor to Event Eight, once again amplifying the platform for free expression without harsh critique. Conversation commenced on separation of art from the artist, on whether it was acceptable to glorify ‘good’ literature regardless of the background of the author or not. Opinions on the fact that social media heavily warped literature & its fandoms were also put forth, while some argued that social 174

Document: SARASWATI-MAGAZINE25.pdf -> Page 175 Content: media accelerated the popularity of various literature pieces. The notion of ill- representation in literature against inclusion of diverse, underrepresented groups also led to in-depth discussion, marking another fruitful T(EA) Series Session. Event X / Closing Session: T(EA) Series Event III The final session of the T(EA) Series concluded the run of the Novel Room for the year as well and experienced its largest offline participation of all time. It was a three hour long session with enthusiastic responses and exciting discussions. Revolving around ‘trash literature’ and glorification of authors such as Colleen Hoover, students offered a variety of opinions around topics such as the authenticity of fan fiction, the justification of controversial stories through banter and uninhibited speech. The session concluded with a humorous and ironic award ceremony for the most unpopular characters, plots and series for categories of which the audience voted upon and decided winners for. The participants were given their final assignment once the session ended. All in all, each session brought in a special verve of energy, and encapsulated the spirit of curiosity and insight of all participants. PHYSICS DISCUSSION CLUB (UNDER THE AEGIS OF THE DEPARTMENT OF PHYSICS) Faculty in Charge: Dr Niyaz Ahmed The Physics Discussion Club holds meetings every Thursday, for one and half hours. The Discussion Club is open to students from all disciplines. The students meet once a week, and one or two of them present a detailed overview, using a PowerPoint presentation, of a research paper or a topic of current research interest. The presentation will include data and graphs and will last approximately 30 minutes. This will be followed by a 10-minute Q&A session, where the speaker’s preparation and understanding of the topic will be thoroughly examined. Students will select the topics they wish to present. Participation in the club is limited to interested students, and each member is required to present when it is their turn. In addition to giving presentations, all members are expected to actively participate in the discussions. After the presentations and discussions, students are welcome to share interesting news, ideas, or books they have recently encountered for group discussion. Learning Objectives: Promote scientific communication and interaction among FY, SY, and TY students. Raise awareness of ongoing research activities worldwide. Make the concepts and equations from the curriculum more engaging and relatable by highlighting their real-world physical significance, moving beyond abstract mathematics. Provide training on effectively extracting valuable information from research papers. Encourage students to brainstorm and identify new research problems for future exploration. Expected learning outcomes Improved ability to communicate physics related topics to both technical and popular audiences. Confidence in using ICT tools and scientific software. Ability to think and select topics for research. Participation in conferences/workshops/research meets. Theme/topics discussed: Quantum Computing and Quantum Information Dark Matter and Dark Energy Gravitational Waves Nanophotonics Superconductivity Particle Physics and the Standard Model Quantum Entanglement and Nonlocality Plasma Physics and Fusion Energy 179

Document: Masters-of-science-in-Chemistry-Department.pdf -> Page 1 Content: Master’s of Science in Chemistry (MSC) A bout the department: The Department of Chemistry at Jai Hind College is led by a team of seven enthusiastic and dedicated faculty members. Each faculty member actively contributes to the undergraduate B.Sc. program through lectures and practical sessions. Five of our faculty members are accredited PG teachers who deliver lectures at various esteemed postgraduate institutions. We foster dynamic learning experiences through interactive teaching methods, research initiatives, and internship opportunities. Our graduates consistently achieve success in renowned institutions, reflecting our commitment to academic excellence. About the HOD: Dr. Brijesh Singh, Ph.D. in Chemistry, is deeply passionate about imparting knowledge in the field of chemistry. He has mentored numerous undergraduate research projects and has co authored several academic papers, frequently collaborating with students. Additionally, Dr. Singh actively contributes to various college committees and boards, demonstrating his commitment to enhancing academic excellence and institutional development. Message from the HOD: The Master’s of Science in Chemistry program is designed to provide you with a rigorous and comprehensive education in various disciplines of chemistry, preparing you for a fulfilling career in research, academia, industry, or beyond. Throughout your studies, you will delve into advanced theoretical concepts and gain hands-on experience in our state-of-the-art laboratories, where you will explore the intricacies of Organic, Inorganic, Physical, and Analytical Chemistry. Additionally, our program places a strong emphasis on communication skills, ensuring you can effectively convey your scientific findings to peers and the broader scientific community. Faculty Details Dr. Brijesh Singh Designation: Head of Department Qualification: Ph.D. Chemistry, M.Sc Experience: 27 Years

Document: Master-of-Science-in-Big-Data-Analytics-department.pdf -> Page 1 Content: Master of Science in Big Data Analytics About the Department : The Department of Msc Big Data Analytics, BSc. IT and B.Voc. in Software Development has recorded consistent improvement in its academic, research and placement performance. We offer a range of innovatively designed programs whose curricula are constantly updated to meet the changing requirements of the industry and also to meet the needs of major stakeholders. Through an innovative teaching-learning process, a teamwork approach and leadership building experience, our students gain vital communication and critical –thinking skills. We believe that our students have been well accepted in their job profiles and have consistently exceeded expectations of the corporate world. The objective of this syllabus is to create a pool of technologically savvy, and upon completion of the M.Sc. in Big Data Analytics program, students will be able to acquire and enhance skills in applied statistics, real analysis and numerical analysis, apply for data analytics job opportunities in the domain of predictive analytics, descriptive statistics, etc. About the HOD: Mr. Wilson Rao is the Post Graduate Controller of Examination and IT Administrator of the college. Along with this, he is also a member of IQAC, Admission Committee, NAAC Criteria IV and Placement Cell Member. Message from HOD: The Master of Science in Big Data Analytics program is a two year full time post graduate program. It comprises four semesters with a total of 88 credits. In collaboration with TCS extends to students rigorously knowledge in big data analytics. The course work contains a compulsory core of professional subjects like statistics, machine learning and enabling technologies for data science relevant to all science disciplines. In order to include practical knowledge on the course, subjects and applications like data, science, analytics, machine learning, business analytics, and programming languages are included in the curriculum of the course.

Document: Eligibility-Criteria-for-Science-Admission-2026–2027.pdf -> Page 1 Content: Eligibility Criteria for Science Admission (2026–2027) NOTE: Being a MINORITY INSTITUTION, reservations for minority quota will be as per Government norms for ALL AIDED and SELF -FINANCED PROGRAMS at UG and PG level. Reservation as per Government norms will also be there under Special Category for Physically Challenged/Sports/Cultural/Ex -Servicemen/Defense quota for ALL AIDED and SELF-FINANCED PROGRAMS at UG and PG level. A candidate for being eligible for admission to an Undergraduate Program leading t o the Bachelor of Science (BSc) degree must have passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate (Std. XII) examination conducted by the Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education or its equivalent from any other Board. Cut-off per centage for Sindhi students (both In -house and Outside) opting for FY (SCIENCE) –MICROBIOLOGY or LIFE SCIENCES as the MAJOR subject in the chosen combination = 60% Cut-off percentage for Non -Sindhi Outsidestudents opting for FY (SCIENCE) – MICROBIOLOGY OR LIFE SCIENCES as the MAJOR subject in the chosen combination = 70% Cut-off percentage for Sindhi students (both In -house and Outside) opting for FY (SCIENCE) –PHYSICS OR CHEMISTRY OR BOTANY OR MATHEMATICS as the MAJOR subject in the chosen combination = 50% Cut-off percentage for Non-Sindhi Outside students opting for FY (SCIENCE) – PHYSICS OR CHEMISTRY OR BOTANY OR MATHEMATICS as the MAJOR subject in the chosen combination = 60%

Document: Eligibility-Criteria-for-Commerce-Admission-2026–2027.pdf -> Page 1 Content: Eligibility Criteria for Commerce Admission (2026–2027) NOTE: Being a MINORITY INSTITUTION, reservations for minority quota will be as per Government norms for ALL AIDED and SELF -FINANCED PROGRAMS at UG and PG level. Reservation as per Government norms will also be there under Special Category for Physically Challenged/Sports/Cultural/Ex -Servicemen/Defense quota for ALL AIDED and SELF-FINANCED PROGRAMS at UG and PG level. A candidate for being eligible for admission t o an Undergraduate Program leading to the Bachelor of Commerce (BCom) degree must have passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate (Std. XII) examination conducted by the Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education or its equival ent from any other Board. Cut-off percentage for Sindhi students (both In-house and Outside) opting for FY (COMMERCE) = 50% Cut-off percentage for Non-Sindhi Outside students opting for FY (COMMERCE) = 90% FIRST YEAR under NEP Subject combinations available in COMMERCE a. Major – Accountancy; Minor – Commerce (Business Management) b. Major – Commerce (Business Management); Minor - Accountancy SECOND YEAR under NEP Option of choosing Accountancy as Major/ Commerce (Business Management) as Minor. OR Accountancy as Minor/Commerce (Business Management) as Major will be available to students based on availability of seats and merit. Once the Major and Minor subjects are selected, no changes will be permitted. THIRD YEAR under NEP Major/Minor combination of subjects will be as opted for in SY. NO INTERCHANGE of MAJOR/MINOR subjects at TY LEVEL.

Document: BDS-BROCHURE-2026.pdf -> Page 2 Content: About Jai Hind College Jai Hind College, Empowered Autonomous, Mumbai, was established in 1948, soon after India’s independence, by a group of distinguished professors from D.J. Sind College, Karachi, along with other visionary educationists. Founded under the Sind Educationists’ Association, the institution was initially set up to support the educational rehabilitation of the Sindhi community. With a commitment to academic excellence, the college empowers students and faculty to realize their potential, uphold discipline, ethics, and moral values, and become well-informed global citizens dedicated to serving society and humanity.

Document: 2nd-Notice-JHC-Common-Entrance-Exam-2026-27.pdf -> Page 1 Content: JAI HIND COLLEGE Basantsing Institute of Science & J. T. Lalvani College of Commerce. and Sheila Gopal Raheja College of Management. Empowered Autonomous NOTICE -II Date: 4th February 2026 Common Entrance Exam (JHC-CEE) for admission to FY Degree (UG) Self Financing Programs for 2026-2027 Syllabus for Online Entrance Exam 2026-27 The Jai Hind College - Common Entrance Exam (JHC-CEE) for admission to the Undergraduate Self -Financing Degree Programmes (BAF,BFM,BBI,BMM,BDS,BAQF) for the academic year 2026 –27 will assess candidates on basic aptitude and analytical ability. The entrance examination will be conducted in online mode and will consist of Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) refer notice dated 27th January 2026 The test will primarily evaluate candidates in the following four areas: English, Logical & Visual Reasoning , Data Interpretation, Mathematics, and Current Affairs, as detailed in the syllabus given below. English  Grammar and Composition (Usage of prepositions/article/conjunctions, Sentence construction/correction, Tenses)  Vocabulary  Communication Etiquette  Reading Comprehension

The mock test will be conducted between 22nd to 27th of April 2026 from 10am to 5pm. Details of Mock test will be sent on the students email id and will also be available on www.jaihindcollege.com PLEASE NOTE – MOCK TEST IS MANDATORY FOR ALL STUDENTS. IV Rules to be followed – before and during the conduct of examination. a. The candidate must appear for the entrance examination from a separate room or location, ensuring that no other person is present in the vicinity for the entire duration of the exam. b. The place should be noise-free with no movement of people in the surroundings. c. All items except simple calculators (non-programmable/ nonscientific) should be removed from the desk. Students are allowed to use battery operated portable calculators. The calculators can be of any type with up to 6 functions, 12 digits and up to two memories. Scientific and programmable calculators are not allowed. d. Candidates must not talk to anyone during the entrance exam period or talk/read to themselves loudly. e. Candidates must not use head phones, earbuds, or any other type of listening equipment except the speaker of the laptop. f. Candidates should ensure that all computer settings are completed & properly tested prior to the exam time. For any failure of the computer system, during the entrance exam period, JaiHind College will not be responsible and the candidate will lose his/her attempt ;no reexamination will be conducted.

Document: First-Notice-JHC-Common-Entrance-Exam-2026-27.pdf -> Page 6 Content: g. Candidates must not switch off the webcam or mic or cover the webcam during the entrance exam. Changing of the camera angles to obstruct views can lead to stopping of the test by the online invigilator or AI enabled system. Candidates must face the computer screen and must not look away from the laptop/desktop. h. Candidates must not communicate with any other person by any means. Candidates must not seek any assistance or refer to study material/mobile/ for answering the questions. i. During the online examination the IP address of the candidate will be tracked to monitor individual participation. This measure is in place to prevent multiple individuals from sitting together and taking the exam simultaneously. It is imperative that each candidate accesses the examination independently, from their own designated location. Candidates adhere strictly to this guideline to avoid any potential issues during the examination. V. Action against candidates found guilty of misconduct/ use of unfair means Candidates are advised in their own interest that they should not furnish any particulars that are false, tampered with or fabricated and should not suppress any material information while submitting online application. At the time of examination or in a subsequent admission procedure, if a candidate is(or has been) found guilty of – (i) Using unfair means or (ii) Impersonating or procuring impersonation by any person or (iii) disclosing, publishing, reproducing, transmitting, storing or facilitating transmission and storage of contents of the test (s) or any information therein in whole or part thereof in any form or by any means, verbal or written, electronically or mechanically for any purpose or resorting to any irregular or improper means in connection with his/ her candidature or (iv) Obtaining support for his/ her candidature by unfair means, or

General Instructions 1. The possibility of occurrence of some problem in the administration of the examination can not be ruled out completely which may impact test delivery and/or result from being generated. In that event, every effort will be made to rectify such problems, which may include movement of candidates, delay in tests. Conduct of a re-exam is at the absolute discretion of the test conducting body i.e. Jai Hind College. Candidates will not have any claim for a re-test. Candidates not willing to move or not willing to participate in the delayed process of test delivery shall be summarily rejected from the process. 2. Decision of Jai Hind College in all matters relating to conduct of Jai Hind College Entrance exam will be final and binding on the candidate. No correspondence or personal enquiries shall be entertained by the College on this behalf. 3. Instances for providing incorrect information and/or process violation by a candidate detected at any stage of the admission process will lead to disqualification of the candidate from the entire admission process. 4. Candidates will take the entrance exam under virtual/online invigilation mode. This is also called the Remote Proctored Exam. 5. Candidates must Login 30 minutes before the entrance exam start time to ensure a smooth process. 6. The exam will be conducted on the 2nd of May 2026 . Slots will be assigned to respective students based on their application number. 7. The candidate has to use his/her user id and Password sent on his/her email for logging

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 2 Content: About the Program The Bachelors in Actuarial Science & Quantitative Finance (BAQF) is an interdisciplinary undergraduate program designed to prepare students for careers in finance, analytics, risk management, insurance and related domains. The key features involve integration of quantitative reasoning with core financial concepts to enable solving real-world challenges in the financial, banking and insurance sectors. Who Should Pursue BAQF? Anyone with a strong aptitude for logical reasoning, analytical mindset and mathematical abilities

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 3 Content: Program Goals Develop expertise in actuarial science and quantitative finance for resolving financial and risk management challenges Foster adaptability with emerging technologies like data science, AI, and fintech in financial contexts Enhance analytical and problem-solving skills using mathematical and statistical methods Promote data-driven decision-making and proactive risk assessment in the financial and insurance sectors Develop strategic and leadership potential through experiential learning Master business writing, professional communication for documentation and stakeholder engagement

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 4 Content: What You’ll Gain Build an integrated skill set to excel in academic and professional certifications. Excel & VBA Financial Modelling & Valuation R Programming Statistical & Actuarial Analysis Python Data Analytics & Automation SQL Database Querying & Management Bloomberg Terminal Market Data & Investment Research Power BI Interactive Data Visualization Risk Management Risk Assessment & Management Team Collaboration Professional Communication Research Skills Financial & Actuarial Research

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 5 Content: Graduates typically begin their careers as Actuarial or Risk Analysts moving further towards positions such as Appointed Actuary, Chief Risk Officer or Consultants. Actuarial Science is a specialised field that applies mathematical and statistical models to evaluate financial risks in banking, investments, insurance, pension funds,and other industries. Actuarial Science India will require over 25,000 new actuaries by 2030, with global demand projected to grow by 300%. Top recruiters include: EY, Pwc, Deloitte, Swiss Re, Milliman, JP Morgan, Goldman Sachs, ICICI Lombard and other global leaders in banking, insurance, consulting, and financial services. Exam Session Year Subject April 2026 1 CS1 + CB2 Sept 2026 2 CB1 + CB3 April 2027 2 CM2 Sept 2027 3 CP2 April 2028 3 CS2 Sept 2028 4 CP1 + CM1 Experience Salary (in INR lakhs) Entry LevelMid LevelSenior Level0102030405060 Proposed timeline for clearing actuarial papers incourse of our BAQF program. Actuarial Salary Progression Scope Growth Curve In India, the actuarial profession is governed by the Institute of Actuaries of India (IAI) and globally by bodies like the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (IFoA), UK. To become a fully qualified actuary with either body, a student must clear a total of 13 professional exams. Our BAQF program is carefully curated to cover 100% academic content of 9 out of the 13 exams.

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 6 Content: Graduates begin as Quant Analysts, Investment Analysts, or Risk Analysts, progressing to roles like Portfolio Manager, Quant Strategist, or Chief Investment Officer. Quantitative Finance combines mathematics, statistics, and computational tools to model financial markets, optimise investments, and manage risk in global financial systems. Quantitative Finance India will require over 1.5–2 lakh new professionals in Quantitative Finance, Risk, and Analytics by 2030, with global demand projected to grow 25%+ in the next 5 years. Top recruiters include Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, BlackRock, Citibank, Axis AMC, and leading global investment firms and hedge funds. Exam Session Year Certification May 2027 2 CFA Level 1 May 2028 3 CFA Level 2 / FRM Part 1 Nov 2028 4 FRM Part 2 / CFA Level 3 Proposed Timeline for CFA and FRM Exams in thecourse of our BAQF program Scope Growth Curve CFAFRMOther Quant Roles Experience Salary (in INR lakhs) Entry Level Mid Level Senior Level0102030405060 Salary Trajectory Across Quant Roles The Quantitative Finance track is academically mapped to CFA® and FRM® certifications—global benchmarks in finance and risk management. Students gain deep exposure to core topics across all 3 CFA levels and both parts of FRM, covering up to 99% of the syllabi, empowering them to attempt these qualifications during the course of the program.

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 11 Content: Teaching-Learning Strategy Judicious integration of interactive lecture sessions by expert faculty and industry professionals with hands-on training through simulations, case studies, and real-world projects. Use of the flipped classroom model maximises engagement with foundational content online, reserving in-person sessions for discussions, problem-solving, and collaborative teamwork. Field visits, mentorship programs, and tools like R, Python, and Excel VBA through coding and software BootCamp ensure practical mastery, while continuous feedback and research-driven capstone projects cultivate critical thinking and innovative ideas essentials for mastery in actuarial science and quant analysis.

Document: JHC-BAQF-Brochure-compressed.pdf -> Page 17 Content: Q1: Is this course only for those pursuing Actuarial exams? No. BAQF supports CFA and FRM aspirants as well. Q2: Can I attempt CFA/FRM while in college? Yes. The structure allows time for certification prep. Q3: Is prior programming knowledge needed? No. Programming tools are taught from the basics. Q4: Will I be placement-ready after graduation? Yes. The program includes internships, projects, and job readiness training apart from placement support. Q5: What are the eligibility criteria? Class XII with Math/Stats. Jai Hind CEE + Interview. Q6: What is the level of math required? Comfort with school-level math and logical reasoning is essential. The course progressively builds complexity. Q7: Is the course open to students from Arts or Commerce background? Students must have taken Mathematics or Statistics in Class 12, irrespective of stream. Q8: Will I get support for applying to international universities after graduation? Yes. Faculty mentors and the placement cell guide students on applications, SOPs, and recommendation letters. Q9: Can I pursue higher studies abroad after BAQF? Absolutely. The curriculum provides a strong foundation for MSc, MFE, or MBA programs globally.

Document: BBA-Brochure.pdf -> Page 5 Content: Admission The BBA (Industry Integrated) program at Jai Hind College, affiliated to University of Mumbai, is designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world business demands. Created in collaboration with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), the program offers a unique blend of academic excellence and industry exposure, with a strong focus on Business Process Management—a key area in today’s enterprise landscape. Students gain hands-on experience in sectors such as banking, finance, insurance, and retail through 100% internship opportunities, many of which lead to full-time job offers. The program also encourages students to pursue globally recognized certifications like CFA and ACCA alongside their degree, enhancing their professional profiles and employability. With its emphasis on practical learning, industry relevance, and professional growth, the BBA (Industry Integrated) program prepares students to thrive in competitive business environments. Admission as per MH- CET guidelines and Government of Maharashtra Email: admissions.commerce.mgmt@jaihindcollege.edu.in P r o g r a m O v e r v ie w Finance &Accountingfor BusinessProcessManagementBanking forBusinessProcessManagement Insurance forBusinessProcessManagement Capital Marketsfor BusinessProcessManagement Retail andMarket Researchfor BusinessProcessManagement Campus toCorporateTransition Agile anddesignthinking Logistics andSupplyChainManagement ManagingBusinessProcesses TCSDesignedPapers
