Document: Bachelors-in-Management-Studies-Brochure.pdf -> Page 5 Content: Introduced by the University of Mumbai in 1999, the Bachelor of Management Studies (BMS) program was designed to meet the growing demand for dynamic management professionals. Today, the BMS program at Jai Hind College stands as a three-year degree or a four-year honours program, comprising 40 comprehensive courses, including a Research Dissertation in the final year. Designed for aspiring managers, entrepreneurs, and future business leaders, the program blends robust theoretical foundations with real-world, application-based learning. With academic autonomy, Jai Hind BMS has adopted a continuous evaluation system—ensuring students are assessed not just by exams, but through fieldwork, internal assessments, group projects, and presentations. This immersive approach gives students the confidence and clarity to thrive in both academic and professional spheres. Document: important-notice-for-abc-registration.pdf -> Page 1 Content: Important Notice for ABC Registration This is to inform all the student that the University Grants Commission, New Delhi and University of Mumbai (Cir.No. DBoEE/ICF/2022 -23/14) have mandated the implementation of Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) from current academic year i.e. 2023-24. As per the National Educational Policy 2020, ABC is a national level facility to promote flexibility of curriculum framework and provide academic mobility to students with appropriate credit transfer mechanism. Therefore, it is mandatory for all the students to generate an ABC ID which needs to be submitted to the university .In order to create ABC IDs students are advised to follow the following steps: Students are hereby informed to follow the following steps to get ABC IDs, they can generate ABC IDs through one of the STEPS given below. STEP A 1)Visit on https://www.abc.gov.in/ 2)Click on my account-> student. 3)For New user – Click on “Sign up for Meri Pehchan” 4)Enter Mobile number, you will get OTP on registered mobile Number 5)Fill all necessary detail and click on verify 6)Students will get ABC ID. Get these details for college record. OR STEP B 1)Sign in to Digi locker by visiting on www.digilocker.gov.in (can be done using Mobile or Aadhar) 2)Go to Search Documents. 3)Go to Education section. 4)Search for ABC Id widget 5)Click on widget to generate and download ABC Id 6)Student will get ABC ID. Get these details for college record. Students are requested that after generating the “ABC. ID” they should Fill the below form

Document: important-notice-for-abc-registration.pdf -> Page 2 Content: https://forms.gle/1ucziouwSNoPHNTQA Please Note The college had circulated a Google Form for the collecting information to create ABC ID’s, if you have filled the form correctly then your ABC ID would have been created, to check your ABC ID you need to Login to your Digi Locker Account with your Aadhaar credentials and there you will find your ABC ID, and accordingly you can login to ABC portal ,Those students who had given wrong data or Mobile Number not linked to the Aadhaar Card or the name provided is not according to Aadhaar Card then your data would have been rejected. So you need to create the ABC ID with one of the Steps given above in the notice. All the students are supposed to enter the Data in the below Form also https://forms.gle/Tnb8ysfYny1TsbAB8 Self Help Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Gw3DUHaJg1c

I also participated in Techsrujan 2024 and cleared the NASSCOM Web Developer Exam in 2023. As part of my technical development, I created QuickCart, a mobile grocery shopping app built using Android Studio, Java, Firebase, and Razorpay. Additionally, I gained hands-on experience as a web developer during a two-month stint with the NGO Real Life Happiness. I am truly grateful for the incredible journey I’ve had at Jai Hind College, which has greatly contributed to my academic and professional growth. These events not only deepened my technical knowledge but also encouraged meaningful interactions with peers and industry experts. Document: MSc-in-Big-Data-Analytics-Brochure-25.pdf -> Page 4 Content: Testimonial The MSc Big Data Analytics program at Jai Hind College has been the ideal fit for me as a student from Statistics background. This program has equipped me with the necessary skills and knowledge to navigate the world of big data. I've gained practical experience working with enormous datasets and using statistical approaches to extract insightful information thanks to the college's cutting-edge data analytics lab and access to industry standard tools. Post Graduating from a prestigious college like Jai Hind gives an opportunity to learn from the best minds. MSc Big Data Analytics at Jai Hind is welldesigned course, very practical and fits perfectly with my background. The course curriculum is curated according to the industry requirement. Faculty members are well qualified and supportive. To sum it all up, it has helped me grow my personality as well as my technical skills. The MSc Big Data Analytics program has really helped me to learn and understand the relevant data skills. The program has a mix of theory and practical concepts. The teachers were very cooperative. The experience will be valuable throughout my career. Further, the MSc in Big Data Analytics is useful for all those people who use data during their internships and jobs. This program teaches you how to handle real- time datasets. It has been an exciting opportunity to pursue an M.Sc. in Big Data Analytics at Jai Hind College. As someone with a B.Sc. in mathematics, I thought this course was an interesting way to connect theory to practical applications. I was first interested in the potential applications of mathematics in data analytics, and this program further ignited my passion for the field. Strong theoretical ideas are coupled with hands-on learning in tools like Python, R, Hadoop, Tableau and Spark in this well-structured curriculum. One of the most valuable aspects of my college experience was the opportunity to appear for interviews with reputed multinational companies such as NielsenIQ. Grateful to be part of Jai Hind College. Document: Jai-Hind-BDS-brochure.pdf -> Page 3 Content: 3 Years Degree and 4 Years Honours Program Program Overview At Jai Hind College, we take pride in offering an intensive 3-year Degree Program in Bachelors of Commerce (Digital Strategy), designed to equip students with the expertise needed to lead and grow online businesses. This cutting-edge curriculum ensures rapid career progression by covering essential digital skills and business strategies demanded by modern brands. Digital Strategy plays a crucial role in today’s business world, integrating technology, marketing, and data analytics to drive growth. It involves leveraging digital platforms, consumer insights, and innovative tools to enhance brand presence, optimize operations, and maximize profitability. The program not only prepares students for dynamic career opportunities but also empowers them to transform traditional family businesses into thriving digital enterprises. With a strong focus on core digital modules, soft skills, and hands-on learning, students gain practical experience through multiple internships aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP).

Page Context: Legacy | Jai Hind College -> Section: Legacy -> Detail: Jai Hind College, Empowered Autonomous, Mumbai, has a history spanning several years since it’s inception in 1948. Soon after the Indian Independence, a group of visionary professors of D.J. Sind College, Karachi, and other eminent educationists founded Jai Hind College under the registered name of ‘Sind Educationists’ Association’ primarily for the educational rehabilitation of the Sindhi community. Their goal was to contribute their expertise, knowledge, and skills in order to set up an educational institution par excellence.

Page Context: Legacy | Jai Hind College -> Section: Legacy -> Detail: From a humble Arts college, Jai Hind College soon diversified into Science in 1949, followed by Commerce in 1980. Post 2000, several Self-financed and Vocational Programs were introduced to improve employability of students, such as Bachelor of Management Studies (BMS), Bachelor of Mass Media (BMM), Bachelor of Accounting and Finance (BAF), Bachelor of Banking and Insurance (BBI), Bachelor of Financial Market (BFM) and, Bachelor of Vocational Studies in Travel & Tourism Management (B. Voc. TTM) and Software Development (B. Voc. SD).Programs such as Bachelor of Digital Strategy (BDS) , Master of Science in Big Data Analytics and Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in collaboration with TCS as the Industry partner,Bachelor In International Accounting (BIA) And Bachelor In Actuarial Science And Quantitative Finance (BAQF) were recently introduced. Inclusion of such Industry aligned Programs are periodically assessed based on feedback from all stakeholders to help align academics with current market demands.

Page Context: Legacy | Jai Hind College -> Section: Legacy -> Detail: Our notable alumni, such as Mr. Sunil Dutt,Mr.Piyush Goyal,Ms.Supriya Sule,Dr.Raghunath Mashelkar,Mr.Vikram Nankani,Mr Ajay Piramal, Ms Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Mr. John Abraham ,Mr. Vishal Dadlani, Mr.Shaan and many others began their illustrious journeys at Jai Hind College, proving that students can achieve national and global recognition with our foundation.

Page Context: Legacy | Jai Hind College -> Section: Legacy -> Detail: The Union Ministry of HRD and the University of Mumbai conferred on us the prestigious Empowered Autonomous Status for a period of ten years from the academic year 2023-24.

Page Context: Legacy | Jai Hind College -> Section: Legacy -> Detail: Over the years , Jai Hind College has empowered students to achieve their dreams while staying true to the value systems of the Founding Fathers.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Since its inception, Jai Hind College (Autonomous) has fervently advocated for fostering an entrepreneurial ecosystem within our society. In line with this vision, the “Entrepreneurship Cell and Skill Hub,” was introduced and digitally inaugurated by our Hon’ble Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi. This initiative is dedicated to empowering job-seekers to become tomorrow’s job-makers through continuous reskilling and hands-on learning experiences. With a keen focus on revitalizing the startup economy, our goal is to bolster the nation’s economic growth. Our vision is to provide aspiring entrepreneurs with the essential platforms and resources needed to transform their innovative ideas into leading businesses of the future, paving the way for groundbreaking advancements and sustainable success.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: One of the only committees on campus that blends business with culture, the best of both worlds. The Jai Hind Business Conclave, a BBA (Bachelors of Business Administration) initiative, is a thrilling two-day corporate event packed with management games, speaker sessions, panel discussions and engaging podcasts.This committee aims at bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real world and provides a unique platform for industry leaders business heads and the policymakers of our country to drive forward innovative ideas and foster a shared vision for a better, brighter and rejuvenated idea. From pre-events at the National Stock Exchange to interaction with the head honchos at Dr. Vaidya, Kelloggs, Deloitte, SBI Mutual Funds and more, JBC has it all.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Founded in the year 2020, the Jai Hind College Incubator & Accelerator Centre is a dedicated space for student startups to thrive. They offer:– Personalized mentoring by industry experts– Modern co-working spaces– Specialized programs to develop entrepreneurial skills– Opportunities to pitch to investorsTheir flagship event, “The Startup Exposition,” offers a platform for startups to showcase their ideas and connect with potential investors. “Intellectus” sessions educate startups on intellectual property rights, while their collaboration with the “Wadhwani Foundation” offers comprehensive support through online and offline programs.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor – Dr. Rakhi SharmaEnactus Jai Hind College, part of the global Enactus network, uses entrepreneurship for social and environmental good. Since joining in 2015, they have launched impactful projects like Tech Shiksha for 2023-2024, which provides education to underprivileged individuals offline. This initiative offers a chance to make a positive societal impact and helps develop a well-rounded skill set, fostering creativity and emotional intelligence.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Project Green Tee, is a quest born from the fraught uncertainties bestowed by the pandemic, to impart opportunities for alternative employment, financial independence, and personal empowerment, to women trudging in fragile hope through the landscape of some parts of Mumbai’s.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: This initiative served two purpose – humanity and sustainability . It featured the participation of this marginalized community to recycle single-use plastic bottles into hand-sewn, stylish recycled t-shirt garments fit for everyday use.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Project Tech Shiksha, launched in December of 2020, was inclined towards slightly different goals. The project served to impart holistic learning to the under-privileged and poverty-ridden youth, whose education had come to a still-stop due to Coronavirus concerns. A significantly huge band of volunteers, with a count spanning in increasing double digits, eagerly volunteered to carry the initiative forward, through customised curriculums conducted over several online classes. In this vein, two distinct sessions on the terrors of plastic waste, as well as the necessity of sustainability, were conducted with the kids involved in the project.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: 120+ kids and women directly impacted and educated via upskilling workshops and educational classes

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Conducted one-on-one classes in technical subjects such as math and science, as well as stimulating workshops in arts and crafts for more than 50 underprivileged children and students who lost access to education as a result of the pandemic.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Jai Hind College strictly prohibits sexual harassment in accordance with the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal) Act, 2013. Sexual harassment is a punishable offense. The college has established an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) to address any issues related to sexual harassment.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor : Dr.Ruchi Chaturvedi

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Zodiak Fest: An annual two-day event where Rotaract Clubs across Mumbai gather for competitions. Day one features ground events, while day two showcases dance and fashion shows, each year themed differently as a legacy project.Silent Saturdays: A month-long initiative held in November, focusing on a selected slum. Each week targets a specific cause—Health, Education, Hunger, and Safety—with activities like street plays every Saturday to raise awareness.RCJC Model United Nations: Held in December, this two-day debate invites college students to discuss international issues across four committees.Cheekh: An International Women’s Day initiative addressing women trafficking and promoting empowerment through awareness and advocacy.World University Fair: Organized by RCJC in collaboration with Jai Hind College’s Career Counseling Cell, featuring 49 universities from around the world for career opportunities abroad.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor – Mrs Sarita Jai Shankar

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Economics Association of Jai Hind College, with over 110 members, provides a platform for students to apply theoretical knowledge practically. Open to all students intrigued by Economics, it hones skills in research, public speaking, and management.Events:Arthanomics: Arthanomics, the annual Economics festival of Jai Hind College was initiated in 2014 by The Economics Association. It’s a festival that gives young, budding economists a chance to experience the multifarious simulations and events relating to economics in the real world. Since its inception, it has always been a platform for college students to display their passion for economics.Annual Economics Symposium: Intra-college event with paper presentations, a movie-making contest, and parliamentary debates.The Contrarian: A student-run journal for perspectives and discourse, collaborating with Arthanomics for events like The Contrarian Column.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Jai Hind College Case Study Cohort, established by the Department of Bachelor of Management Studies, aims to find practical and real modern-day solutions to business problems through intensive analysis and creative problem-solving. Supported by faculty and industry mentors, the Cohort promotes case-based learning and problem-solving.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Milestones AchievedJai Hind College represented India at the HSBC/HKU Asia Pacific Business Case Competition 2017 in Hong Kong. The team, including Upamanyu Acharya, Diveek Jain, Pratik Malhotra, and Ishaan Singh, along with Faculty Advisor Dr. Rakhi Sharma, reached the quarter finals, becoming a Top 16 team globally. Representing India, which had one of the most competitive national competitions of any of the teams in Asia Pacific, Jai Hind College is proud of their achievement and hopes to build on our legacy to help next years’ teams progress even further.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Business TrainingThe Case Study Cohort focuses on training students to adopt a business mindset and solve real-world cases analytically. Sessions include insights from mentors, faculty, and industry specialists, promoting hands-on business discussions and solution generation.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Case Study Cohort-Media and NewsletterThe Cohort creates fresh, informative content across platforms like Instagram, LinkedIn, and Facebook. Their weekly newsletter, CSC Weekly, covers business themes, corporate news, insights, and recommendations in a visually engaging format.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor : Dr. Reema Julka

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: All political science students are members of this association.The Political Science Forum organizes events and talks to inform students about important national and international issues. In the 2014-15 academic year, the forum hosted a guest lecture on ‘Human Rights’ by Dr. Rashmi Oza and a talk on the “Socio-Political & Economic Dimensions of Globalization” by Mr. Arun Ferera. The popular ‘Youth Parliament’ event provided students with insights into parliamentary proceedings. Ten of our students were selected by Mumbai University for the state-level competition.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor- Dr. Ruchi Chaturvedi

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Psychology is one of the most sought after courses at Jai Hind College. The Psychology Association offers budding psychologists a platform to gain new perspectives and develop their skills. It fosters unity within the department and engages students with events like Chitrakatha, Manthan, and Mindscape. Through passionate leadership and diverse activities, the association attracts many students from across the other courses of the college.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Ms. Safina Rakhangi

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Throughout the years, Jai Hind College’s Nature Club has been actively engaging students in various environmental initiatives. Beginning with a memorable Nature Trail to Uttan in December 2013, accompanied by Mr. Udhav, Ms. Leena, and Dr. Devangi, the club sparked enthusiasm among commerce students for nature observation. In February 2014, a screening of the BBC documentary “Broken Tail” raised awareness about tiger conservation, emphasizing their crucial role in ecosystems. Further activities included a talk on environmental pollution and plastics in August 2014, a tree plantation drive in collaboration with CSR and NSS, and a sale of medicinal plants in September. The club’s upcoming excursion to Vishnubaug in Badlapur promises further exploration of Maharashtra’s rich flora and fauna, continuing its commitment to environmental education and conservation efforts.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor- Ms. Safina Rakhangi

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The History Association of Jai Hind College is a dynamic group comprising faculty and elected students, fostering both academic and real-life skills. Notable activities from 2018-2019 include a research methodology workshop by Dr. Preeta Nilesh, field visits to the CSMVS museum with interactive sessions on Indian heritage, community outreach projects with various NGOs, and a gender sensitization workshop featuring the film “Boys who like Girls” followed by discussions and documentary production on consent. In 2019-2020, the association organized an Annual Student Seminar on “History from the Lens of Indian Cinema” and participated in a RUSA-sponsored conference at Xavier’s College, with students presenting papers selected for peer-reviewed publication.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Dr. Reshma Jaisinghani

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Jai Hind College Accountancy Association highlights the significance of accounting, current industry trends, and the opportunities for accountancy students. Focused on enhancing learning beyond the classroom, the Association hosts a variety of events throughout the year. Each year, four distinguished events are meticulously organized—two per semester—spanning seminars, interactive sessions, and more. At the heart of these initiatives lies Elevation, the Association’s flagship event. This grand one-day affair is a celebration of international accounting day, featuring a mix of activities, engaging games, quizzes, seminars to offer students an unparalleled and enriching educational experience.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor- Dr.Balakrishna

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Their goal is to foster holistic student development through community service. The N.S.S Unit engages in numerous social events and projects in collaboration with organizations and NGOs. These initiatives include addressing civic issues with BMC, assisting civilians at police stations, promoting women empowerment and gender equality, conducting cleanliness and blood donation drives, and raising awareness about cancer, AIDS, hepatitis, road safety, and anti-tobacco campaigns.They host the socio-cultural program “UMEED” in partnership with an NGO to spread awareness and happiness. Their involvement extends to participating actively in rallies, seminars, workshops, and inter-collegiate competitions.Additionally, they enthusiastically celebrate Independence Day and Republic Day and organize a seven-day residential camp.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Ms.Simmin Bawa

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Philosophy Association started in the academic year 2014-15. The aims and objectives of the association are as follows:● Promoting awareness of current ethical dilemmas.● Fostering the spiritual growth of each student.● Cultivating a critical and logical perspective on life.● Advancing knowledge and combating ignorance.● Helping each student discover their full potential.● Advocating for a balanced and wholesome lifestyle

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Women’s Development Cell is a dedicated initiative focused on addressing the holistic needs and aspirations of female students. By organizing a variety of activities throughout the year, the cell aims to empower women, promote their well-being, and create a supportive environment.The cell prioritizes the physical and mental health of female students by providing information and resources on critical issues such as cervical cancer, first aid, nutrition, and anemia.Through events like Nari Mukti Divas, the cell cultivates a platform for women’s voices to be heard, promoting self-esteem, confidence, and leadership skills.The cell’s annual Nari Mukti Divas celebration serves as a cornerstone of its efforts, bringing together the student community to celebrate women’s achievements and address pressing issues through creative expressions like performances and plays.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor : Ms Mahek Punjabi
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor – Mrs. Yasmin Singaporewala

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: ConCourse is the dynamic guest lecture series and speakers’ committee of the BAF, BBI, and BFM departments at Jai Hind College. It brings industry leaders like Craig Travasso, Tarang Gupta, Guru Gauranga Das, Sanjay Singh, Malay Damania, Nikunj Biyani, and many more to share insights on diverse topics like entrepreneurship, finance, ethical hacking, corporate crisis management, motorsport fundamentals, and mental health. Designed to go beyond textbooks, ConCourse offers interactive sessions that provide real-world knowledge, networking opportunities, and career guidance. By blending education with industry exposure, it equips students with the skills and perspectives needed to navigate their professional journeys with confidence.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Launched in June 2021, this club immerses students in filmmaking through screenings, discussions, and projects. It helps them learn film analysis, production, and storytelling, deepening their media skills.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor – Mr. Saiprasad Shetty / Ms Naziya Khan

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Constellation is a distinguished media conclave organized by the students of Jai Hind College’s Mass Media Department. It offers a deep dive into the media industry through interactive sessions with experienced professionals.Over the past editions, Constellation has included industry insiders like actors Adil Hussain, Amole Gupte, Saahil Shroff alongside artist Manish Kharage and writers like Mayank Tewari and Rahul Desai. It aims at covering diverse fields such as Improv and Public speaking, AI Specialisation, Public Relations, Journalism, Costume Design, Meme Marketing, and more. Constellation is committed to creating the most compelling and informative mass media conclave in the college for the media’s excellence and curious student minds.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Jai Hind College’s BMM department’s annual Mass Media festival is ‘Detour.’ An extravaganza with a legacy of over one and a half decade, ‘Detour’ promises a blend of friendly competition, fun and an abundance of memories (in the form of DSLR images and video montages). It encompasses a variety of events such as dance, drama, band, fashion show, quizz, J.A.M., etc. as well as enjoyable pre-events like the arrival of the ‘minions’ of ‘Despicable Me 4’ in the Jai Hind Campus (2024) and social activities to give back to the community.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Mr. Wilson Rao

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Dot Com Club organized an outstanding tech fest this academic year, Cyberstrike 2025, with the theme “Genesis and Exodus.” The event was a perfect combination of technical, gaming, and cultural activities that attracted participants and audiences alike. The fest featured thrilling gaming competitions, including popular titles like Valorant, FIFA, BGMI, and VR games, offering an exciting platform for gamers to showcase their skills. On the cultural side, there were engaging events such as a ramp walk, canvas painting sessions, and an intriguing escape room that added a creative and interactive element to the fest.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: In addition to the fest, the club also demonstrated its commitment to social responsibility through E-Waste 2024, a CSR initiative that aimed to raise awareness about electronic waste management. As part of this initiative, the club successfully collected an impressive 454 kg of e-waste from institutes across South Bombay. The activity was further enriched by informative seminars and conferences, providing valuable insights into the importance of sustainable practices and environmental conservation. The combination of innovative events and meaningful initiatives made this year’s activities by the Dot Com Club truly remarkable.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Entourage is the premier intercollegiate financial festival of Jai Hind College’s Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM. True to its name, Entourage brings together the brightest financial and creative minds for two days of intense competition, strategic thinking, and corporate exposure. With engaging pre-events and a decade-long legacy, the fest challenges participants to navigate real-world financial complexities while showcasing their innovation, problem-solving skills, and cultural talents. More than just a competition, Entourage is an exciting platform for learning, networking, and pushing boundaries in a dynamic and electrifying environment.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Family Managed Business Hub provides a centralized platform for collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and networking among family-owned enterprises. It serves as a supportive ecosystem where these businesses can address common challenges, exchange insights, and foster synergies. Such a hub facilitates the continuity and growth of family businesses by offering resources, mentorship, and a sense of community. Additionally, it contributes to the preservation of family values and legacies, creating a robust network for mutual support and shared success.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor- Dr.Kruti Pandya

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Department of Biotechnology held its annual festival, Genaces 2023—Bioflix, on January 25, 2023, promoting scientific temperament and entrepreneurship among students. Coordinated by S.Y. B.Sc. students with support from F.Y. and T.Y. students, it featured activities like The Big Shot Game Show and Bioheist, drawing over 140 participants. Special guest Ms. Krishma Shah, cofounder of Clinibiz and a Forbes-featured young entrepreneur, added to the event’s success.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Green Club of Jai Hind College is a student-led organization dedicated to promoting environmental awareness and sustainability. Through initiatives like “Plantonate,” a plant donation drive, the club actively engages students and the community in creating a greener future. Their focus spans from encouraging the cultivation of medicinal and ornamental plants to fostering a deeper connection with nature. The Green Club not only educates about environmental issues but also empowers individuals to take concrete actions towards sustainability. By organizing various activities and campaigns, they strive to make a positive impact on campus and beyond, inspiring a new generation of eco-conscious citizens. Their commitment to a safer, cleaner, and more sustainable environment reflects the club’s vision of a healthier planet for all.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Jai Hind College Gymkhana, established by founder Mr. Shivdasani, is the vibrant hub of sports at Jai Hind College. It oversees various sporting events throughout the academic year, including the prestigious Athletic Meet and the highly anticipated intra-college sports extravaganza, Sport-O-Mania. With a rich tradition of fostering sportsmanship, the Gymkhana promotes physical fitness and competitive spirit through activities like cricket, badminton, tug of war, volleyball, football, basketball, throwball, handball, kabaddi, athletics, swimming, and many individual events.

It may also organize career fairs, workshops, and networking events to connect students with industry professionals. By helping students secure meaningful internships, the cell plays a vital role in enhancing their practical skills, building professional networks, and improving their employability upon graduation.Medicinal and ornamental plants to fostering a deeper connection with nature. Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Kalki: A Cultural Gathering is the intercollegiate arts festival of the English Association, founded and manifested for the first time on September 11th and 12th, 2015.Kalki emerged from a collective, deep desire to create a college festival that truly allowed every person to explore his or her creativity in a non-judgmental space. The festival’s spirit was the elimination of competition and it was designed with two days of deep and spontaneous exploration of theatre, music, fine art, film and literature.For its inaugural year, Kalki hosted a large number of people. The festival partnered with some of Mumbai’s best cultural groups and distinguished personalities, and floored viewers who got the chance to interact with industry stalwarts.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Chairperson – Ms. Neesha Vinchu

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Reading Room Committee oversees various activities aligned with the library. Led by the Librarian, the committee includes Dr. Sangeeta Godbole, Mr. Uddhav Zharekar, Ms. Divya Bhatnagar, Ms. Shilpa More, and Ms. Cellina Fernandes as ex-officio members, with Ms. Karen D’souza serving as Secretary. Activities such as book displays, a book donation drive with 900 books showcased and 645 donated, and the annual Fiction Fest highlight their calendar. The committee also hosts the Digital Ink competition, expanding it to an intercollegiate level, fostering literary creativity among students.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor – Mr. Saiprasad Shetty

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Also started in June 2021, this club focuses on writing and storytelling. It offers book discussions, writing workshops, and literary events, enhancing students’ narrative skills and written communication.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Created in June 2021, this club uses media for social impact. It runs community projects and awareness campaigns, allowing students to address social issues and drive positive change.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Established in June 2021, this club explores media and live performance. It includes theater, improvisation, and multimedia projects, helping students develop stagecraft and audience engagement skills.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor : Ms.Kajal Ramchandani

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Sindhi Circle, Jai Hind College is the cultural wing of the department of Sindhi Language formed to promote Sindhi Language through talented Sindhi Students.They organise a nationwide event with the name of ‘HOJAMALO’ in which individuals of all age groups are expected. Their event attracts 25+ colleges and 200+ participants each year from all the colleges within and outside Mumbai.HOJAMALO is an intercollegiate event organised by Sindhi Circle for the promotion of Sindhi language and culture. A number of colleges’ teams come to show their talents through fashion show, dance and drama competitions.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Co-Chairpersons: Dr. Archana Ashtekar and Mr. Fazal Shahzaman
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Dr. Archana Ashtekar and Mr. Fazal Shahzaman

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Social and Dramatic Union (SDU) is the premiere cultural body of Jai Hind College, and serves as a powerhouse of creativity, spirit, and student leadership. As the leading cultural committee, SDU not only nurtures artistic talent within the college but also proudly represents Jai Hind at inter-collegiate cultural festivals across Mumbai, bringing home accolades and making a mark wherever we go. SDU also gives a chance to students across all streams to learn working as a team and exhibit their management skills in organising various events.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Throughout the year, SDU curates some of the most awaited events on campus. These include the fun-filled annual Fresher’s Day, Rose Day, Teacher’s Day Celebration, Children’s Day, the exciting theme launch, Achievers’ Nite , and finally, SHOUTT (Show Us The Talent), our flagship inter-collegiate festival that brings together hundreds of students to compete, collaborate, and showcase their talents. With vibrant performances, powerful dramatics, literary excellence, and artistic flair, SDU creates platforms where students don’t just perform, they shine.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Talaash, hosted by Jai Hind College’s BMS department, is one of the biggest management fests in Mumbai. Over 24 years, it has evolved into a major intercollegiate event showcasing student talents.Talaash 2023-2024 featured management challenges, informal events, and sports, along with music, drama, talent shows, and a fashion show judged by esteemed personalities. This year added a unique twist to cultural activities, celebrating student creativity and passion with support from sponsors.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Ms. Yasmin Singaporewala

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Endeavour, the Editorial Club of the Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM at Jai Hind College, was established in 2018 with the vision of fostering critical reading, writing, and research skills essential for the corporate world. Committed to creating insightful and relevant content, Endeavour serves as a platform for students to stay informed about current events beyond textbooks, bridging the gap between academia and real-world developments. Through collaboration and intellectual engagement, the club nurtures a community that encourages analytical thinking, effective communication, and professional growth.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Advisor: Ms.Anupama

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Literary and Debating Society at Jai Hind College fosters a vibrant platform for students passionate about literary arts. Through debates like ‘Teachers vs Students’ and ‘Junior vs Senior College,’ it encourages inclusive dialogue and critical thinking. The society hosts workshops by professionals, expanding learning beyond textbooks, and organizes Public Speaking and Elocution Competitions year-round. It serves as a free-spirited haven for all literary and debate enthusiasts, nurturing creativity and community at Jai Hind College.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Jai Hind College’s Training & Placement Cell serves as the cornerstone for student career development. With strong faculty support, the cell connects students to premium internships and placements, focusing on cultivating industry-ready professionals. The cell’s mission is to equip students with essential technical knowledge, soft skills, and confidence necessary for career success. To achieve this, they offer tailored skill development workshops covering crucial areas such as CV building, communication, body language, and interview strategies. Students benefit from hands-on internship experiences with leading companies, gaining practical insights into their chosen fields. The cell’s robust recruitment drives, featuring over 100 prestigious companies, provide students with diverse placement opportunities. Through these comprehensive efforts, Jai Hind College ensures its students are well-prepared to excel in their professional journeys.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Promotion of Indian Classical Music, dance and Culture

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: About SPICMACAY (Society for the Promotion of Indian Classical Music and Culture Amongst Youth) is a non-political, nationwide, voluntary movement founded in 1977 by Dr. Kiran Seth, Professor-Emeritus at IIT, Delhi who was awarded the ‘Padma Shri’ for his contribution to the arts in 2009. SPICMACAY’s intention is to enrich the quality of formal education by increasing awareness about different aspects of Indian heritage and inspiring the young mind to imbibe the values embedded in it. It is an apolitical, non-profit, voluntary movement, committed to increasing awareness of Indian culture & classical art forms, Committed to Preserving & promoting India’s intangible heritage with focus on Youth and Students, rooted in Schools & Colleges to enhance quality of education through arts.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: It seeks to inspire youth through experiencing the mysticism embodied in the rich and heterogeneous cultural tapestry of Indian and World Heritage, with a hope that the beauty, grace, values, and wisdom embodied in these arts will influence their way of life and thinking and inspire one to become a better human being.
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: About the SPIC MACAY Heritage Club

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The primary aim of the club is to raise awareness about the diverse facets of Indian culture, including its regional variations, folk traditions, and indigenous practices. It encourages students to immerse and know about the rich heritage and encourage them to adopt interest in art and creativity which is important for holistic growth of the students. The club seeks to delve into the lesser-known aspects of Indian arts and folk styles, shedding light on their historical significance and contemporary relevance and encourage students to volunteer to take up activities to save art and artists. Through curated events and workshops, the club aims to showcase the beauty and complexity of Hindustani music and contemporary Indian art forms, fostering a deeper appreciation among the students. To enrich the cultural landscape of the college, the club organizes live performances, lecture demonstrations, and interactive sessions with renowned artists and scholars. Recognizing the importance of both tangible artifacts and intangible cultural practices, the club endeavors to safeguard India’s rich heritage for future generations.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The above Student Civic Engagement Club is dedicated to fostering a sense of community responsibility and active citizenship among students. Through workshops and seminars, the club provides civic training that educates members about their rights and responsibilities in a democratic society. Life skills programs are a core component, offering practical training in areas such as financial literacy, effective communication, and leadership. The club also organizes various volunteering opportunities, partnering with local nonprofits and community organizations to allow students to apply their skills in real-world settings. These activities not only benefit the community but also help students build a robust network and gain valuable experiences. Overall, the club aims to create well-rounded individuals who are prepared to contribute positively to society. Self Defense Course , Yoga Course and First Aid Course was brought under this.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Ministry of Tourism celebrated this year at India@75 by launching the campaign focusing on “YUVA Tourism Club” with the strategic objective of creating interest, awareness and a sense of responsibility towards our country’s natural and cultural heritage / tourism amongst students and youth. The vision is to nurture and develop young ambassadors of Indian tourism who would become aware of tourism possibilities in India, appreciate our rich cultural heritage and develop an interest and passion for tourism. These young ambassadors at Jai Hind College will be managed by an Executive team from students of B.Voc Travel and Tourism Management along with interested students from all streams who would be catalysts in promoting sustainable tourism practices, experiences and heritage in India and in the region.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Established in 2019 as an initiative by the Finance Department, Baazaar has grown into the financial hub of Jai Hind College, expanding beyond its original scope. While the monthly magazine remains central to its efforts, Baazaar now fosters financial literacy through engaging social media content, interactive events, and finance-related resources, making complex concepts accessible and engaging. Committed to bridging the gap between theory and real-world financial trends, Baazaar ensures students stay informed about the ever-evolving financial landscape.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Market Mindset, an initiative by Jai Hind College’s BAF, BBI, and BFM departments, promotes financial literacy through workshops, discussions, and interactive sessions. With initiatives like the 50-Day Financial Literacy Series, it equips students with market insights and investment strategies. Under the guidance of Ms. Yasmin Singaporewala, the Panel Discussion on Finance & Entrepreneurship on November 26th featured esteemed speakers Aditya Vengar, Khushi Shah, Akshat Garg, and Aaditya Thorat, moderated by Mr. Craig Travasso. The session provided valuable insights on entrepreneurship and financial growth, bridging the gap betweenacademics and real-world finance

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: In Tune In Touch is a dynamic platform at Jai Hind College that fosters creativity and provides students with opportunities to showcase their talents. With a strong team working behind the scenes, the society brings its vision to life through performances, community outreach, and interactive workshops with industry professionals, offering valuable insights into the creative world. Events like the In Tune In Touch Premier League strengthened team spirit, while the year culminated in a grand finale, celebrating months of dedication and artistic expression. As it continues to grow, In Tune In Touch remains committed to pushing creative boundaries and creating unforgettable experiences.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Big Bank Theory is an initiative by the Self-Financed Department of Jai Hind College, dedicated to enhancing financial literacy and providing students with a deeper understanding of Banking and Insurance. Through expert-led sessions, industry visits, and interactive learning experiences, this initiative bridges the gap between academic knowledge and real-world financial applications. By offering hands-on exposure to key industry concepts, The Big Bank Theory equips students with the skills and insights needed to navigate the evolving financial landscape.

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG (LIFE) at Jai Hind College is a student-led initiative by the Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM, dedicated to integrating profitability with purpose in modern finance. LIFE promotes Impact Finance and ESG Investing through practical applications, including The Impact Auction, where participants analyze companies from an ESG perspective in a dynamic bidding challenge. The initiative also hosts The LIFE Podcast, featuring insights from industry leaders like Ms. Purva Dafle, providing real-world perspectives on sustainable finance. Through interactive events and expert discussions, LIFE at Jai Hind College equips students with the skills to navigate the evolving financial landscape responsibly
Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: Faculty Chairperson: Gokul Ganesan

Page Context: Cells and Societies | Jai Hind College -> Section: Cells & Societies -> Detail: The Chemical Society of Jai Hind College offers a vibrant platform for students to explore, learn, and engage with the fascinating world of chemistry. Through initiatives like ChemTalk, students connect with experts and gain insights into contemporary developments in the field. In addition, regular workshops and training sessions on sophisticated analytical instruments and industry relevant skills are organised to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world applications. Beyond academics, the Society actively engages in awareness and outreach programs, emphasizing the role of chemistry in everyday life and its potential to address societal challenges. With a balanced focus on knowledge sharing, skill development, and social responsibility, the Chemical Society strives to inspire a new generation of chemists equipped to make meaningful contributions to science and society.

Document: glimpses-from-the-global-e-summit-2023.pdf -> Page 2 Content: I Will & I Can ABOUT JAI HIND COLLEGE (AUTONOMOUS), MUMBAI "To be the institution of choice for students and employers alike, known for producing good citizens and leaders by providing a well-rounded education of international standards", and our students & faculty together realize the same. The motto of the college, I will and I can, inspires us, and is manifested in the form of a proactive culture as we continue to push boundaries wherever we can. Established in 1948, Jai Hind College (Autonomous) is one of the premier educational institutions in Mumbai. OUR MISSION

Document: glimpses-from-the-global-e-summit-2023.pdf -> Page 3 Content: Elixirs are magical potions that promise immortality. They need to be carefully formulated, with just the right excipients! Entrepreneurship is very similar. It takes a disruptive idea, backed by driven founders and unerring resources to become immortal in the corporate world. The Jai Hind College Global Entrepreneurship Summit is an Academic Conference and a National Level Start-up Competition where we reach out to over 150 Start-ups to come compete. It is an ecosystem where people get to network and interact with multiple business and industry experts for mentoring and funding opportunities. Along with this, we also conduct skill enhancing workshops and business competitions for undergraduate students who can test and improvise on their existing skills. 10th & 11th February, 2023 Jai Hind College, Mumbai was conducted on

Document: CONCOURSE.pdf -> Page 3 Content: Concourse is an initiative taken by the department of BAF, BBI, and BFM of Jai Hind College. It aims to push the boundaries of the protected environment our students grow within before they launch themselves into the world. It is a committee that strives to ensure the personal and professional development of the students. The team invites professionals from various fields of work to interact with the students on important topics. Aside from conducting sessions, the team also organizes seminars and workshops every year. Whether it be the struggles of navigating the corporate world, finance jargon, or overcoming personal obstacles, embarking on careers in entertainment, and sports, ConCourse has you covered. ABOUT US

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 1 Content: BY DEPARTMENT OF BAF, BBI AND BFM A Recap of the Ultimate Finance Fest

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 2 Content: Organised by the Department of BAF, BBI, and BFM at Jai Hind College, Entourage has continuously evolved over the years and become one of the most prominent financial intercollegiate fests in South Bombay. Entirely managed by the students of Jai Hind College, every year it runs under the administration of a dedicated core committee. The fest first gained prominence in 2014, and later in 2015, the theme Infinity and Beyond took it to new heights. Its 10th edition was celebrated in 2021 focusing on merging finance, innovation, and creativity. Today, Entourage is more than just a festival. It is a platform for learning, networking, and showcasing talents that resonate with the aspirations of every participant. With each passing year, it raises the bar higher, leaving an unforgettable mark in the realm of college festivals.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 3 Content: Eras Incarnate E N T O U R A G EE N T O U R A G E 30 th January, 20 25 D e p a r t m e n t o f B A F B B I B F MD e p a r t m e n t o f B A F B B I B F M Entourage 2025 is all set to blend the tapestry of eras into a unified fabric of reality, as we reveal to you an untold collaboration of the past, present and future! Decipher the legacies of yesterday while envisioning the marvels of tomorrow, as we create a path that transverses through the realistic timescape. Together, let's embark on this timeless voyage where every ripple defines the evergreen flow of time.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 4 Content: Entourage helps you by providing you with an incredible opportunity to learn and gain financial knowledge. It teaches you skills you didn't know you needed while also being entertaining and engaging. Our motto is to provide students with a platform to implement their knowledge & skills practically in an informative, fun & creative way. Financial fests challenge everyone's capability to face the corporate world and at the same time evolve as a person. Entourage has always been a hub for students to learn and grow, and I am confident that it will continue to be so in the future and make a name for itself in the world of finance.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 5 Content: "Entourage" is organized by a Chairperson, Vice Chairpersons leading the five core departments, and Heads of Departments managing specialized sub-teams.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 12 Content: The theme reveal pre-event for Entourage 2025 was an immersive experience that brought the festival’s essence to life. Students and participants embraced the theme by dressing as representations of the past, present, and future, adding a creative and engaging touch to the event. The excitement kicked off with a high-energy flashmob, setting the stage for the grand reveal. Following the reveal, the event featured a series of interactive games that encouraged participation and team bonding. To conclude the day, a jamming session created a lively atmosphere where attendees enjoyed music and informal networking. The pre- event successfully built anticipation for Entourage 2025, setting the tone for the festival’s dynamic and innovative spirit.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 13 Content: The Entourage CL Meet served as a vital preparatory session for contingent leaders, providing a comprehensive overview of the upcoming festival. We commenced with a focused presentation, outlining the fest's schedule, key themes, and overall structure, ensuring all CLs grasped the fundamental framework. Following this, we delved into the event highlights, showcasing the unique features and competitive aspects of flagship events, accompanied by a clear explanation of judging criteria and specific event rules. A thorough breakdown of the fest's rules and regulations, including the code of conduct, disciplinary guidelines, and logistical procedures, was provided to ensure a fair and organized environment. A dedicated Q&A session facilitated open communication, allowing CLs to seek clarifications and address any concerns directly with the organizing committee. To foster networking and encourage interaction among the diverse college representatives, we concluded the meet with a brief and engaging icebreaker activity, promoting a sense of camaraderie and collaborative spirit. This session aimed to equip CLs with the necessary knowledge and tools to ensure their contingents had a smooth and enriching experience at Entourage.

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 15 Content: The Vault of Doom event was a structured debate competition centered around two distinct topics. The first debate focused on "Freedom 251," where teams argued for and against the subject. The debate format included opening statements, witness testimonies with a creative "pink envelope" twist, a "Block and Battle" question session, and a rapid-fire round. The second debate tackled the "Lalit Modi Case," following a similar structure with opening statements, witness testimonies (again with the pink envelope twist), and a "Block and Battle" question session. The event aimed to test participants' debating skills, strategic thinking, and ability to adapt to unexpected challenges. EVENT 1Vault of Doom

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 16 Content: This financial simulation event combined real-world stock market trading with strategic gameplay. Participants were given a virtual budget of ₹3 lakhs to invest in companies like Reliance, HDFC Bank, and Tata Steel, making quick trading decisions within a limited time. A unique Snakes and Ladders element added an extra layer of strategy, where answering finance-related questions correctly helped players advance while incorrect answers led to setbacks. The competition also introduced real-world market challenges like a virus outbreak and crude oil price surges, testing adaptability and risk management skills. The event successfully merged financial acumen, decision-making, and market analysis into an engaging and competitive experience. EVENT 2The Serpent’s Ascent

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 17 Content: The "Statement of Affairs" event was a two-round competition designed to test financial acumen and strategic thinking. In the first round, "The Departmental Dash," participants faced a dynamic challenge involving rotating departmental questions. They had to quickly adapt as they switched between departments, showcasing their ability to think on their feet and manage time effectively under pressure. The second round, "The Imposter Challenge," shifted the focus to strategic analysis and advocacy. Finalists analyzed a complex case study, identifying a fake department ("imposter"), and then persuasively argued for their assigned department. This round emphasized critical thinking, persuasive argumentation, and sound judgment under pressure, all while working independently. EVENT 3Statement of affairs

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 18 Content: Auction Impossible, the opening event of Entourage 2025, challenged participants to make strategic investment decisions in a high- stakes fictitious company auction. Each team was given a budget of ₹10 crores and had to acquire at least three companies through competitive bidding. Over four intense rounds, teams engaged in calculated bidding wars, adjusting their strategies as company returns were revealed after each round. What began as cautious investments quickly turned into a dynamic financial battle, with teams balancing risk and reward. The event concluded with a sense of excitement and anticipation, successfully setting the tone for the festival while emphasizing investment strategy, market analysis, and decision-making under pressure. EVENT 4Auction Impossible

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 19 Content: The "Cash Me If You Can" event was a strategy-focused competition that brought together 8 teams to test their business acumen. Each team was given 75,000 in starting capital and assigned a specific industry, and they were tasked with increasing their wealth through strategic decision-making, negotiation, and risk management. The game was played on a board, and teams rolled dice to move around the board, buying cities, trading assets, and navigating chance events. The game simulated real-world business, and teams had to make critical decisions about when to buy, sell, and trade assets. They also had to be aware of the risks involved in each decision, as chance events could have a significant impact on their wealth. The "Cash Me If You Can" event was a resounding success, and it provided participants with a valuable learning experience. The event taught participants about the importance of strategic thinking, negotiation, and risk management. It also gave them a taste of the challenges and rewards of the business world. EVENT 5Cash Me If You Can

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 20 Content: Too Many Layers, Too Little Time was a fast- paced event that blended creativity, agility, and time management in an NFT design challenge. Participants had a base time of 10 minutes to create an original NFT using Canva, but they could earn additional minutes by competing in mini-games like hopscotch, bottle flipping, and memory challenges. As teams raced to secure extra time, the room buzzed with energy and strategic thinking. Once the design phase began, participants worked under intense pressure to bring their ideas to life. The event concluded with submissions being evaluated by judges, making for a thrilling finish. Combining artistic innovation with time- based strategy, the event was a standout highlight of Entourage 2025. Too Many Layers, Too Little time

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 21 Content: A captivating escape room event that challenged participants' wit and ingenuity. Teams embarked on a thrilling journey through time, navigating four distinct rooms: the Past, Present, Future, and Mystery. In each room, participants were tasked with uncovering five hidden clues to unlock a 5-digit code, granting them passage to the next stage of their chronological adventure. The "Past" room immersed players in historical artifacts and events, requiring them to decipher codes based on ancient civilizations or historical figures. The "Present" room focused on current affairs and social trends, challenging participants to solve puzzles related to contemporary challenges. The "Future" room presented a vision of the world yet to come, with puzzles involving technological advancements and hypothetical scenarios. Finally, the "Mystery" room offered a unique and unpredictable experience, testing players with mind-bending riddles and unexpected twists. Chambers of Deception

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 22 Content: PitchCraft was a flagship marketing event that challenged participants to reinvent existing products with a creative twist. Teams created a 2-minute advertising video and delivered a 2-minute pitch to showcase their product’s market potential. Some standout ideas included Cadbury Toothpaste, a fun twist on oral care; Starbucks Immunity Booster, a caffeine- free energy drink; and Cutlery Suite by Swarovski, a luxury dining product. The event tested marketing creativity, strategic thinking, and persuasive communication, highlighting the power of branding in transforming everyday products. Pitchcraft

Document: ENTOURAGE-2025.pdf -> Page 24 Content: On January 31st, 2025 (day 2), Jai Hind College in Churchgate, Mumbai, hosted the South Regional Zone Quiz for Varsity, a prestigious finance quiz competition organized by Entourage and Zerodha Varsity. The event saw an overwhelming response with over 240 registrations and a turnout of more than 500 participants. Aspiring finance enthusiasts from across the region gathered to test their knowledge and compete for prizes worth 3.5 lakh. The quiz was conducted in partnership with QSHALA, a renowned quizzing platform. This exciting competition provided a platform for students to showcase their understanding of financial concepts and demonstrate their passion for the world of finance.

Document: Baazaar-2024-25.pdf -> Page 18 Content: Cash Couture brought the thrill of finance and deception into play with two dynamic events— Celebrity Stock Exchange and Mafia Game, each reimagined with financial twists. In Celebrity Stock Exchange, students traded shares of celebrities, with market values fluctuating based on real-time news flashes. These updates acted as catalysts, influencing whether players should buy, sell, or hold onto their celebrity stocks, mirroring the unpredictability of real-world stock markets. Strategic decision-making and quick reactions were key to maximizing profits. Meanwhile, the classic Mafia Game was given a financial makeover—scammers replaced the Mafia, auditors took the role of the Police, and citizens became unsuspecting investors. This version challenged participants to detect fraud, audit suspicious transactions, and outwit financial tricksters. By combining elements of finance, strategy, and deception, Cash Couture offered an engaging experience that tested both wit and market intuition, making financial literacy fun and interactive. CASH COUTURE

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 1 Content: AN INITIATIVE BY THE DEPARTMENTS OF BAF, BFM AND BBI ANNUAL REPORT 2024-25 FACULTY ADVISOR - Prof. Yasmin Singaporewala

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 2 Content: ABOUT US The Market Mindset is an initiative dedicated to fostering financial literacy among students by providing them with the knowledge and skills necessary to navigate the world of investing. Designed as an interactive and educational platform, the program equips participants with a solid foundation in financial markets, investment strategies, and wealth-building principles. Through expert-led discussions, hands-on learning experiences, and an engaging 50-day journey, The Market Mindset aims to bridge the gap between theoretical finance and real- world application. Our mission is to inspire students to develop a strategic approach to money management and empower them to make informed financial decisions for a secure future.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 3 Content: AIM The Market Mindset program aims to: Spread Financial Awareness – Introduce students to finance, investing, and market dynamics. Bridge Theory and Practice – Provide expert insights and real-world financial knowledge. Encourage Active Participation – Engage students in a 50-day journey exploring the Indian stock market. Promote Informed Investing – Teach strategic decision-making for smarter investments. Build a Strong Financial Mindset – Foster disciplined money management and long- term wealth planning.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 4 Content: OUR WHATSAPP COMMUNITY The Market Mindset WhatsApp Community serves as an interactive platform where students can engage in discussions, seek guidance, and stay updated on financial trends. Key Features of Our Community: • Daily Market Insights – Regular updates on stock market trends, economic developments, and investment opportunities. • Expert Guidance – Direct access to insights from professionals in the finance industry. • Interactive Discussions – A space for students to ask questions, share opinions, and learn collaboratively. • Exclusive Learning Resources – Curated articles, videos, and tools to enhance financial knowledge. • Live Q&A Sessions– Opportunities to clarify doubts and gain deeper insights into investing strategies. This community serves as an extension of The Market Mindset program, ensuring that students stay engaged and continue their financial learning journey beyond formal sessions.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 5 Content: TOPICS COVERED IN THE 50-DAY JOURNEY The Market Mindset’s 50-day journey covered essential financial and investment concepts, including: Stock Market Basics – Market structure, stock exchanges, and key terms. Investment Strategies – Short-term vs. long-term investing, value and growth strategies. Technical & Fundamental Analysis – Stock charts, indicators, and financial statements. Risk Management – Market volatility, diversification, and minimizing losses. Power of Compounding – Long-term wealth building through reinvestment. Financial Statements – Understanding balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow. Economic Indicators – Impact of GDP, inflation, and interest rates on markets. Portfolio Building – Asset allocation for different risk appetites. Investment Mistakes – Case studies on common errors and lessons learned. Final Review & Next Steps – Evaluating progress and future financial planning. This journey provided students with practical knowledge and hands-on experience to make informed financial decisions.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 6 Content: THE INTRODUCTORY SESSION The Market Mindset program began with an Introductory Session on November 26, 2024, at the Jai Hind College auditorium, attended by 500 students. The session introduced key financial concepts and set the stage for the 50-day journey through an engaging panel discussion. Panel Discussion & Speakers Moderated by Mr. Craig Travasso, the panel featured: • Mr. Aaditya Iyengar – Forbes Top 100 Digital Star, CFA Charterholder. • Ms. Khushi Shah – Founder of The Baseline, former Morgan Stanley summer analyst. • Mr. Akshat Garg – AVP-Research at Choice, CFA Charterholder. • Mr. Aditya Thorat – Assistant Manager at BlinkX by JM, NISM VIII Certified. Key Topics Discussed Financial Markets – Essentials for new investors. Money Mindset – Developing smart investment habits. Power of Compounding – Long-term wealth-building strategies. Portfolio Building – Balancing investments wisely. Investment Mistakes – Learning from losses. The session provided students with a strong foundation in finance and investing. The interactive Q&A allowed them to engage directly with experts, clarifying doubts and gaining practical insights. The session successfully introduced the 50-day journey, generating 450+ student registrations and sparking enthusiasm for deeper financial learning.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 7 Content: EVENT HIGHLIGHTS A glimpse into The Market Mindset’s journey—insightful panel discussions, engaging interactions, and the dedicated team behind it all.

Document: THE-MARKET-MINDSET-1.pdf -> Page 8 Content: ACKMOWLEDGEMENTS We sincerely appreciate everyone who contributed to making The Market Mindset a success. This initiative brought together industry experts, eager participants, and a dedicated team to create an enriching learning experience in financial literacy and investing. The journey was filled with insightful discussions, interactive sessions, and impactful takeaways. A special thank you to our organizing team for their hard work and commitment. For any insights, follow us: https://www.instagram.com/themarketmindset.jhc? igsh=anMyemo5NHd3cWdh https://chat.whatsapp.com/DJjOadIbEFjE3bSv3jCAKm For any queries, contact: PARAM MISHRA, HEAD OF THE MARKET MINDSET COMMITTEE, 9170353951.

Document: BIG-BANK-THEORY.pdf -> Page 3 Content: MESSAGE FROM THE COORDINATOR The Big Bank Theory offers a unique platform for students of Jai Hind College to acquire comprehensive knowledge and insights into the banking and insurance sectors. It presents an unparalleled opportunity for students to delve into the practical applications of the concepts acquired in their studies, bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and real-world experience. Our mission is to impart an in-depth understanding of the Banking and Insurance industries, empowering students to apply their skills in practical ways. As a centre for knowledge and development, the Big Bank Theory is positioned to become one of the most informative and innovative drives, offering a unique blend of knowledge and creativity that sets it apart. -YASMIN SINGAPOREWALA

Document: BIG-BANK-THEORY.pdf -> Page 4 Content: ABOUT US Welcome to the Big Bank Theory , an educational event organized by the SYBBI at Jai Hind College. We are a dedicated cohort of students, committed to sharing our knowledge and insights into the realm of finance. Our mission is to enhance the understanding of managing personal finances within the Banking and Insurance spectrum. This drive has been created to simplify complex concepts into clear, practical advices that individuals can incorporate into their financial decisions, whether it is selecting the most appropriate savings plan, understanding insurance policies, or effectively navigating the banking system. We are excited to start this path of learning and growth and extend an invitation to you as we take proactive steps to achieve improved financial literacy. We will be organizing a variety of activities such as financial quizzes and talks with intellectuals.

Document: BIG-BANK-THEORY.pdf -> Page 5 Content: WHAT ISBIG BANK THEORY? and opportunity for participants to learn more about Banking and Insurance offerings through Q&A sessions. Furthermore, the "Big Bank Theory" involves tasks such as quizzes, case studies, and simulations which enhance learning by making it enjoyable and effective. At the conclusion of the event, participants will have improved abilities to make knowledgeable financial choices, comprehend the operations of banks and insurance firms, and value the importance of financial planning in their personal lives. The "Big Bank Theory" is a captivating and informative event aimed at increasing participants' knowledge of Banking and Insurance. Targeting students, professionals, and the general public, this event seeks to simplify the intricate realm of finance through interactive sessions, workshops, and expert lectures. Participants can gain knowledge on important subjects like financial literacy, banking processes, insurance investments, and the significance of insurance in protecting assets. The event usually includes panel talks with top figures in the industry, hands-on demonstrations,

Document: BIG-BANK-THEORY.pdf -> Page 6 Content: ABOUT BANKING AND INSURANCE Banking and Insurance is a specialized undergraduate program that specifically targets the financial services sector, with a focus on banking and insurance. The aim of this three-year program is to provide students with a thorough understanding of banking operations, insurance laws, financial management, and investment tactics. BBI graduates are equipped for jobs in banking, insurance, financial consulting, and other related fields. The program blends theoretical learning with hands-on experience through internships, case studies, and projects, preparing graduates as versatile professionals to tackle challenges in the financial sector. The degree provides a strong base for future academic pursuits, such as pursuing a master's in finance, MBA, or obtaining professional certifications like CFA, ACCA or CFP to name a few.

Document: BIG-BANK-THEORY.pdf -> Page 7 Content: GOALS OF THE EVENT Build Industry Connections: Facilitate networking opportunities by connecting students with professionals from the Banking and Insurance industries through guest lectures or Q&A sessions. Address Common Misconceptions: Clarify any misconceptions students may have about Banking and Insurance careers, highlighting the reality of working in these fields. Educate Students on Banking and Insurance: Provide a clear overview of what Banking and Insurance are, including key concepts, services, and the roles they play in the economy. Explain Career Opportunities: Outline various career paths in the banking and insurance sectors, including required qualifications, skills, and potential career progression. Demonstrate Practical Skills: Teach students essential skills and knowledge related to banking operations, such as account management, loan processes, and insurance policies. Enhance Financial Literacy: Equip students with basic financial literacy skills, such as budgeting, saving, and understanding interest rates.
