Document: LIFE-Report.pdf -> Page 3 Content: About Us Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG (LIFE) at Jai Hind College is a student-led initiative, where members see Finance through the lens of Impact Investing & ESG. It is where we redefine our Financial understanding by aligning Profitability with Purpose. With the global financial ecosystem rapidly progressing, evolving & innovating we at LIFE truly believe that ESG & Impact Investing is the next big revolution in the financial world especially within the Indian Markets. We dive into the world of Impact Investing and ESG principles with activities that combine leadership, sustainability, and financial smarts. LIFE aims to advocate for profitable opportunities that make a difference via all its projects & activities. Practical Projects, Industry Partnerships & Developing skills by Interacting with Professionals are the means to boost our knowledge. LIFE is redefining the student perspective towards finance by aligning purpose with profitability!!

Document: LIFE-Report.pdf -> Page 5 Content: ESG Meme Competition Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG organised an ESG Meme Competition which made learning fun bringing in the dimension of memes. Students from all across the college participated creating & submitting memes relating to ESG, depicting truths, busting myths and showcasing facts with a hind of laughter and sarcasm. This was unique way to spread awareness and get students to think about what ESG means and its potential impact in today’s financial landscape with a Gen-z flavour.

Document: LIFE-Report.pdf -> Page 6 Content: Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG organized - The Impact Auction where teams from Various Colleges entered the world of finance and sustainability with a twist of auction dynamics. Teams stepped into the shoes of an Impact Investment Fund as teams - bid for a diverse set companies modelled to represent real-world enterprises, analyzing their Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) metrics to maximize returns while embracing sustainability. Through exciting rounds filled with twists, surprises, & strategic decision- making. This event challenged their analytical and bidding skills leading to them sharpening concepts whilst also gaining knowledge about how ESG concepts are applied Companies from over 40+ industries were covered as part of the auction giving students a great opportunity to think from multiple dimensions Impact Auction

Document: LIFE-Report.pdf -> Page 7 Content: Industry Interaction Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG organized an Industry Interaction with - Mr. Ramraj Pai - Former President of CRISIL Ltd. Mr. Ramraj Pai was the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) at the Impact Investor’s Council (IIC) India’s preeminent not-for-profit advisory body focused on building the ecosystem for impact investing in the country. He is a credit market expert, with a specialization in financial services and inclusion related issues and he served as the President at CRISIL limited where he drove a variety of initiatives over a 24-year career including heading up CRISIL’s flagship credit rating businesses also starting up and scaling a separate division for ratings of small and medium enterprises in India. Apart from his vast professional experience & financial prowess, he holds a dynamic personality and his fondness to connect, empower & interact with today's youth is what led him to interact with students from college. Over 100+ students had the opportunity to interact with Ramraj Sir wherein he busted myths & gave students a detailed overview in the of Impact Investing space. The session also allowed students to get answers to their questions regarding Impact Investing & gave them a sense how they can balance Profits with Purpose. Meeting such an industry stalwart truly gave students an amazing networking opportunity while giving them a real-world perspective of what Finance is really like!!

Document: LIFE-Report.pdf -> Page 9 Content: Leaders in Impact Finance & ESG values & appreciates the contribution of all our student-members who have taken up this leadership initiative upon themselves to promote Impact Investing & ESG which is the future of the finance Landscape!! We at LIFE sincerely thank everyone who has contributed in making LIFE a successful initiative!! Follow us on - life_jhc (Instagram Handle) Acknowledgements

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Autonomy Structure -> Detail: Jai Hind College has successfully undergone three cycles of NAAC accreditation and placed consistently in the ‘A’ Grade. In the last (third) cycle, it has been a proud recipient of A++ grade with a CGPA score of 3.52 out of 4. In adherence to the recommendations of the NAAC peer team, the College submitted its proposal for grant of autonomy following which the Union Ministry of HRD and the University of Mumbai conferred the Autonomous Status on the institution from the academic year 2018-19 onwards. The University of Mumbai will confer the graduation and post-graduation degree which will now also bear the name of Jai Hind college.

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Autonomy Structure -> Detail: Subsequently, the University of Mumbai Management Council vide Resolution (Item 04) dated 27th June, 2023, conferred the prestigious Empowered Autonomous Status to Jai Hind College for a period of ten years from academic year 2023-24 to 2032-33.

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: Jai Hind College has been conferred the Autonomous Status by the Union Ministry of HRD and the University of Mumbai from the academic year 2018-19 onwards. The aims and objectives of the Institution in taking this giant step towards enhancement of overall excellence under Academic Autonomy are as follows:

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: To design its own curricula and syllabi in keeping with the global trends so as to provide world-class education;
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: To use modern pedagogical tools to achieve greater effectiveness in the teaching-learning process;
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: To evolve innovative methods to assess learning outcomes based on higher order thinking skills (HOTS);
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: To nurture creativity and out-of-the-box thinking;

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: To be a platform to cultivate conscientious citizenship through promotion of community services, extension activities and the like, as an integral part of the curricula for the benefit of society at large.

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: The College has held several brainstorming sessions with its faculty, the pivotal architect in this mammoth process, and has outlined the following goals under autonomy:
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: Development of subject-specific career-oriented skills
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: Search of possible solutions to environmental and ecological problems
Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Goals & Objectives -> Detail: Stress on interdisciplinary activity

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Structure Under Autonomy -> Detail: Jai Hind College Autonomous is governed by mandated Statutory Boards which include the Governing Body, Academic Council, Finance Committee, the Board of Studies of individual subjects and the IQAC which ensure reinforcement of the quality mechanisms outlined in the goals and objectives of the Institution. Based on the policy framework set by these Statutory Committees, the day-to-day administrative activities are carried out under the direction of the Head of the Institution, the Principal, and in consultation with the Academic Heads of Faculties, the Vice Principals and the Heads of various Departments as well as several administrative committees. The policies framed by these committees are based to a large extent on the feedback received from the various stakeholders involved in the overall educational process.

Page Context: Autonomy Page | Jai Hind College -> Section: Programs & Credits -> Detail: For the successful completion of a program, a student is required to acquire 150 academic credit points along with 2 mandatory credits under AAA or ‘Additional Academic Activity’ across a period of three years of an undergraduate program. A student can also acquire 2 additional credits through non-mandatory AA or ‘Additional Activity’ as well as 2 non-mandatory credits through Internships during the course of the entire program. These non-mandatory credits will not be added to the final Grade point of the student but will be reflected in his/her Grade card. Whereas the AAA courses are more of a co-curricular nature to equip the student towards learning beyond the curriculum, the AA courses are more of an extra-curricular nature including Extension activities, CSR activities etc.
